Robotics Daily Papers

Daily papers related to Robotics, Reinforcement Learning, Vision-Language Models, World Models, LLMs, VLA, and VLN

March 17, 2026

Towards Generalizable Robotic Manipulation in Dynamic Environments

Abstract (English): Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models excel in static manipulation but struggle in dynamic environments with moving targets. This performance gap primarily stems from a scarcity of dynamic manipulation datasets and the reliance of mainstream VLAs on single-frame observations, restricting their spatiotemporal reasoning capabilities. To address this, we introduce DOMINO, a large-scale dataset and benchmark for generalizable dynamic manipulation, featuring 35 tasks with hierarchical complexities, over 110K expert trajectories, and a multi-dimensional evaluation suite. Through comprehensive experiments, we systematically evaluate existing VLAs on dynamic tasks, explore effective training strategies for dynamic awareness, and validate the generalizability of dynamic data. Furthermore, we propose PUMA, a dynamics-aware VLA architecture. By integrating scene-centric historical optical flow and specialized world queries to implicitly forecast object-centric future states, PUMA couples history-aware perception with short-horizon prediction. Results demonstrate that PUMA achieves state-of-the-art performance, yielding a 6.3% absolute improvement in success rate over baselines. Moreover, we show that training on dynamic data fosters robust spatiotemporal representations that transfer to static tasks. All code and data are available at https://github.com/H-EmbodVis/DOMINO.

摘要（中文）: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型在静态操作中表现出色，但在具有移动目标的动态环境中表现不佳。这种性能差距主要源于动态操作数据集的稀缺，以及主流 VLA 模型对单帧观测的依赖，限制了其时空推理能力。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了 DOMINO，一个用于可泛化动态操作的大规模数据集和基准，包含 35 个具有层级复杂度的任务、超过 11 万条专家轨迹以及一个多维度评估套件。通过全面的实验，我们系统地评估了现有 VLA 模型在动态任务中的表现，探索了用于动态感知的有效训练策略，并验证了动态数据的泛化性。此外，我们提出了 PUMA，一个动态感知型 VLA 架构。通过集成以场景为中心的历史光流和专门的世界查询来隐式地预测以物体为中心的未来状态，PUMA 将历史感知与短时程预测相结合。结果表明，PUMA 达到了最先进的性能，在成功率上比基线模型取得了 6.3% 的绝对提升。此外，我们证明了在动态数据上进行训练可以培养鲁棒的时空表征，并迁移到静态任务中。所有代码和数据均可在 https://github.com/H-EmbodVis/DOMINO 上获取。

TLDR: The paper introduces DOMINO, a large-scale dataset and benchmark for dynamic robotic manipulation, and PUMA, a dynamics-aware VLA architecture, demonstrating improved performance and generalizability in dynamic environments.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了DOMINO，一个用于动态机器人操作的大规模数据集和基准测试，以及PUMA，一个动态感知的 VLA 架构，展示了在动态环境中改进的性能和泛化能力。

Authors: Heng Fang, Shangru Li, Shuhan Wang, Xuanyang Xi, Dingkang Liang, Xiang Bai

From Passive Observer to Active Critic: Reinforcement Learning Elicits Process Reasoning for Robotic Manipulation

Abstract (English): Accurate process supervision remains a critical challenge for long-horizon robotic manipulation. A primary bottleneck is that current video MLLMs, trained primarily under a Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) paradigm, function as passive "Observers" that recognize ongoing events rather than evaluating the current state relative to the final task goal. In this paper, we introduce PRIMO R1 (Process Reasoning Induced Monitoring), a 7B framework that transforms video MLLMs into active "Critics". We leverage outcome-based Reinforcement Learning to incentivize explicit Chain-of-Thought generation for progress estimation. Furthermore, our architecture constructs a structured temporal input by explicitly anchoring the video sequence between initial and current state images. Supported by the proposed PRIMO Dataset and Benchmark, extensive experiments across diverse in-domain environments and out-of-domain real-world humanoid scenarios demonstrate that PRIMO R1 achieves state-of-the-art performance. Quantitatively, our 7B model achieves a 50% reduction in the mean absolute error of specialized reasoning baselines, demonstrating significant relative accuracy improvements over 72B-scale general MLLMs. Furthermore, PRIMO R1 exhibits strong zero-shot generalization on difficult failure detection tasks. We establish state-of-the-art performance on RoboFail benchmark with 67.0% accuracy, surpassing closed-source models like OpenAI o1 by 6.0%.

摘要（中文）: 精确的过程监控仍然是长程机器人操作的一个关键挑战。一个主要的瓶颈在于，当前主要在监督微调 (SFT) 范式下训练的视频多模态大语言模型 (MLLM) 作为被动的“观察者”发挥作用，识别正在发生的事件，而不是根据最终任务目标评估当前状态。在本文中，我们引入了 PRIMO R1 (过程推理引导的监控)，一个将视频多模态大语言模型转化为主动“评论员”的 7B 框架。我们利用基于结果的强化学习来激励显式的思维链生成，以进行进度估计。此外，我们的架构通过显式地将视频序列锚定在初始状态和当前状态图像之间，来构建一个结构化的时间输入。在所提出的 PRIMO 数据集和基准测试的支持下，跨不同领域内环境和领域外真实世界人形机器人场景的大量实验表明，PRIMO R1 实现了最先进的性能。定量地讲，我们的 7B 模型实现了专业推理基线平均绝对误差的 50% 降低，表明相对于 72B 规模的通用多模态大语言模型，相对准确度得到了显著提高。此外，PRIMO R1 在困难的故障检测任务中表现出强大的零样本泛化能力。我们在 RoboFail 基准测试上建立了最先进的性能，准确率为 67.0%，超过了像 OpenAI o1 这样的闭源模型 6.0%。

TLDR: The paper introduces PRIMO R1, a reinforcement learning framework that transforms video MLLMs into active "Critics" for process supervision in robotic manipulation, achieving state-of-the-art performance and strong zero-shot generalization.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了PRIMO R1，一个强化学习框架，将视频 MLLM 转变为主动 "评论家"，用于机器人操作中的过程监督，实现了最先进的性能和强大的零样本泛化。

Authors: Yibin Liu, Yaxing Lyu, Daqi Gao, Zhixuan Liang, Weiliang Tang, Shilong Mu, Xiaokang Yang, Yao Mu

MA-VLCM: A Vision Language Critic Model for Value Estimation of Policies in Multi-Agent Team Settings

Abstract (English): Multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) commonly relies on a centralized critic to estimate the value function. However, learning such a critic from scratch is highly sample-inefficient and often lacks generalization across environments. At the same time, large vision-language-action models (VLAs) trained on internet-scale data exhibit strong multimodal reasoning and zero-shot generalization capabilities, yet directly deploying them for robotic execution remains computationally prohibitive, particularly in heterogeneous multi-robot systems with diverse embodiments and resource constraints. To address these challenges, we propose Multi-Agent Vision-Language-Critic Models (MA-VLCM), a framework that replaces the learned centralized critic in MARL with a pretrained vision-language model fine-tuned to evaluate multi-agent behavior. MA-VLCM acts as a centralized critic conditioned on natural language task descriptions, visual trajectory observations, and structured multi-agent state information. By eliminating critic learning during policy optimization, our approach significantly improves sample efficiency while producing compact execution policies suitable for deployment on resource-constrained robots. Results show good zero-shot return estimation on models with differing VLM backbones on in-distribution and out-of-distribution scenarios in multi-agent team settings

摘要（中文）: 多智能体强化学习（MARL）通常依赖于一个中心化的评论家来估计价值函数。然而，从零开始学习这样的评论家非常耗费样本，并且常常缺乏跨环境的泛化能力。与此同时，在互联网规模数据上训练的大型视觉-语言-动作模型（VLA）展现出强大的多模态推理和零样本泛化能力，但直接将其部署于机器人执行仍然在计算上是令人难以想象的，尤其是在具有不同形态和资源约束的异构多机器人系统中。为了解决这些挑战，我们提出了多智能体视觉-语言-评论家模型（MA-VLCM），该框架用一个经过微调的预训练视觉-语言模型来替代MARL中学习的中心化评论家，以评估多智能体行为。MA-VLCM充当一个中心化的评论家，其以自然语言任务描述、视觉轨迹观测和结构化的多智能体状态信息为条件。通过消除策略优化期间的评论家学习，我们的方法显著提高了样本效率，同时生成了适用于部署在资源受限机器人上的紧凑型执行策略。结果表明，在多智能体团队环境中，使用不同VLM骨干的模型在同分布和异分布情况下都表现出良好的零样本回报估计。

TLDR: The paper introduces MA-VLCM, a framework that replaces the learned centralized critic in MARL with a fine-tuned vision-language model, improving sample efficiency and enabling deployment on resource-constrained robots by leveraging VLMs for multi-agent behavior evaluation conditioned on language task descriptions.

简要说明: 该论文提出了MA-VLCM，一个使用微调的视觉-语言模型替代MARL中学习的中心化评论器的框架。通过利用VLM进行多智能体行为评估，并以语言任务描述为条件，从而提高样本效率，并能够在资源受限的机器人上部署。

Authors: Shahil Shaik, Aditya Parameshwaran, Anshul Nayak, Jonathon M. Smereka, Yue Wang

NavThinker: Action-Conditioned World Models for Coupled Prediction and Planning in Social Navigation

Abstract (English): Social navigation requires robots to act safely in dynamic human environments. Effective behavior demands thinking ahead: reasoning about how the scene and pedestrians evolve under different robot actions rather than reacting to current observations alone. This creates a coupled prediction-planning challenge, where robot actions and human motion mutually influence each other. To address this challenge, we propose NavThinker, a future-aware framework that couples an action-conditioned world model with on-policy reinforcement learning. The world model operates in the Depth Anything V2 patch feature space and performs autoregressive prediction of future scene geometry and human motion; multi-head decoders then produce future depth maps and human trajectories, yielding a future-aware state aligned with traversability and interaction risk. Crucially, we train the policy with DD-PPO while injecting world-model think-ahead signals via: (i) action-conditioned future features fused into the current observation embedding and (ii) social reward shaping from predicted human trajectories. Experiments on single- and multi-robot Social-HM3D show state-of-the-art navigation success, with zero-shot transfer to Social-MP3D and real-world deployment on a Unitree Go2, validating generalization and practical applicability. Webpage: https://github.com/hutslib/NavThinker.

摘要（中文）: 社交导航要求机器人在动态的人类环境中安全行动。有效的行为需要前瞻性思考：推断场景和行人如何在不同的机器人动作下演变，而不是仅仅对当前的观察做出反应。这产生了一个耦合的预测-规划挑战，即机器人动作和人类运动相互影响。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出NavThinker，一个具有未来意识的框架，它将一个动作条件化的世界模型与在策略强化学习相结合。世界模型在Depth Anything V2补丁特征空间中运行，并执行未来场景几何结构和人类运动的自回归预测；然后，多头解码器生成未来的深度图和人类轨迹，从而产生一个具有未来意识的状态，该状态与可通行性和交互风险对齐。至关重要的是，我们使用DD-PPO训练策略，同时通过以下方式注入世界模型的前瞻性思考信号：（i）动作条件化的未来特征，融合到当前的观察嵌入中，以及（ii）来自预测的人类轨迹的社交奖励塑造。在单机器人和多机器人Social-HM3D上的实验表明了最先进的导航成功率，并实现了到Social-MP3D的零样本迁移以及在宇树Go2上的真实世界部署，验证了泛化能力和实际适用性。 网页：https://github.com/hutslib/NavThinker。

TLDR: NavThinker proposes an action-conditioned world model for social navigation, coupling future prediction with reinforcement learning, demonstrating superior performance in simulated and real-world environments.

简要说明: NavThinker 提出了一个用于社交导航的动作条件世界模型，将未来预测与强化学习相结合，并在模拟和真实环境中展示了卓越的性能。

Authors: Tianshuai Hu, Zeying Gong, Lingdong Kong, XiaoDong Mei, Yiyi Ding, Qi Zeng, Ao Liang, Rong Li, Yangyi Zhong, Junwei Liang

AnoleVLA: Lightweight Vision-Language-Action Model with Deep State Space Models for Mobile Manipulation

Abstract (English): In this study, we address the problem of language-guided robotic manipulation, where a robot is required to manipulate a wide range of objects based on visual observations and natural language instructions. This task is essential for service robots that operate in human environments, and requires safety, efficiency, and task-level generality. Although Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs) have demonstrated strong performance for this task, their deployment in resource-constrained environments remains challenging because of the computational cost of standard transformer backbones. To overcome this limitation, we propose AnoleVLA, a lightweight VLA that uses a deep state space model to process multimodal sequences efficiently. The model leverages its lightweight and fast sequential state modeling to process visual and textual inputs, which allows the robot to generate trajectories efficiently. We evaluated the proposed method in both simulation and physical experiments. Notably, in real-world evaluations, AnoleVLA outperformed a representative large-scale VLA by 21 points for the task success rate while achieving an inference speed approximately three times faster.

摘要（中文）: 本研究探讨了语言引导的机器人操作问题，即要求机器人能够根据视觉观测和自然语言指令来操作各种物体。这项任务对于在人类环境中运行的服务型机器人至关重要，并要求具备安全性、效率和任务层面的通用性。虽然视觉-语言-动作模型（VLA）已在该任务上展现出强大的性能，但由于标准Transformer骨干网络计算成本高昂，它们在资源受限环境中的部署仍然具有挑战性。为了克服这一限制，我们提出了一种轻量级VLA——AnoleVLA，它使用深度状态空间模型来高效处理多模态序列。该模型利用其轻量且快速的序列状态建模能力来处理视觉和文本输入，从而使机器人能够高效地生成轨迹。我们在仿真和物理实验中对所提出的方法进行了评估。值得注意的是，在真实环境评估中，AnoleVLA在任务成功率方面比一个具有代表性的大规模VLA高出21个百分点，同时推理速度快了大约三倍。

TLDR: AnoleVLA is presented as a lightweight vision-language-action model using deep state space models. It achieves higher task success rates and faster inference speeds than large-scale VLAs in real-world robotic manipulation tasks.

简要说明: AnoleVLA是一个轻量级的视觉-语言-动作模型，它利用深度状态空间模型。在真实世界的机器人操作任务中，其任务成功率更高，推理速度比大型视觉-语言-动作模型更快。

Authors: Yusuke Takagi, Motonari Kambara, Daichi Yashima, Koki Seno, Kento Tokura, Komei Sugiura

AutoMoT: A Unified Vision-Language-Action Model with Asynchronous Mixture-of-Transformers for End-to-End Autonomous Driving

Abstract (English): Integrating vision-language models (VLMs) into end-to-end (E2E) autonomous driving (AD) systems has shown promise in improving scene understanding. However, existing integration strategies suffer from several limitations: they either struggle to resolve distribution misalignment between reasoning and action spaces, underexploit the general reasoning capabilities of pretrained VLMs, or incur substantial inference latency during action policy generation, which degrades driving performance. To address these challenges, we propose \OURS in this work, an end-to-end AD framework that unifies reasoning and action generation within a single vision-language-action (VLA) model. Our approach leverages a mixture-of-transformer (MoT) architecture with joint attention sharing, which preserves the general reasoning capabilities of pre-trained VLMs while enabling efficient fast-slow inference through asynchronous execution at different task frequencies. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks, under both open- and closed-loop settings, demonstrate that \OURS achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art methods. We further investigate the functional boundary of pre-trained VLMs in AD, examining when AD-tailored fine-tuning is necessary. Our results show that pre-trained VLMs can achieve competitive multi-task scene understanding performance through semantic prompting alone, while fine-tuning remains essential for action-level tasks such as decision-making and trajectory planning. We refer to \href{https://automot-website.github.io/}{Project Page} for the demonstration videos and qualitative results.

摘要（中文）: 将视觉-语言模型（VLMs）集成到端到端（E2E）自动驾驶（AD）系统已显示出改进场景理解的潜力。然而，现有的集成策略存在若干局限性：它们要么难以解决推理空间和动作空间之间的分布不匹配问题，要么未能充分利用预训练VLMs的通用推理能力，要么在动作策略生成过程中产生显著的推理延迟，从而降低驾驶性能。为了解决这些挑战，我们在本文中提出了\OURS，一个端到端的AD框架，它将推理和动作生成统一在一个单一的视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型中。我们的方法利用了一种具有联合注意力共享的混合Transformer（MoT）架构，它保留了预训练VLMs的通用推理能力，同时通过在不同任务频率下的异步执行，实现了高效的快慢推理。在多个基准数据集上，在开放循环和闭环设置下的广泛实验表明，与最先进的方法相比，\OURS实现了具有竞争力的性能。我们进一步研究了预训练VLMs在AD中的功能边界，考察了何时需要针对AD的微调。我们的结果表明，预训练VLMs可以通过语义提示来实现具有竞争力的多任务场景理解性能，而微调对于决策和轨迹规划等动作级别任务仍然至关重要。我们参考\href{https://automot-website.github.io/}{项目页面}以获取演示视频和定性结果。

TLDR: The paper introduces AutoMoT, an end-to-end autonomous driving framework that uses a unified vision-language-action model with a mixture-of-transformer architecture for efficient reasoning and action generation, showing competitive performance on various benchmarks.

简要说明: 这篇论文介绍了一种名为AutoMoT的端到端自动驾驶框架，该框架使用统一的视觉-语言-动作模型和混合Transformer架构，以进行高效的推理和动作生成，并在多个基准测试中表现出竞争性的性能。

Relevance: (10/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Authors: Wenhui Huang, Songyan Zhang, Qihang Huang, Zhidong Wang, Zhiqi Mao, Collister Chua, Zhan Chen, Long Chen, Chen Lv

D-MEM: Dopamine-Gated Agentic Memory via Reward Prediction Error Routing

Abstract (English): Autonomous LLM agents require structured long-term memory, yet current "append-and-evolve" systems like A-MEM face O(N^2) write-latency and excessive token costs. We introduce D-MEM (Dopamine-Gated Agentic Memory), a biologically inspired architecture that decouples short-term interaction from cognitive restructuring via a Fast/Slow routing system based on Reward Prediction Error (RPE). A lightweight Critic Router evaluates stimuli for Surprise and Utility. Routine, low-RPE inputs are bypassed or cached in an O(1) fast-access buffer. Conversely, high-RPE inputs, such as factual contradictions or preference shifts, trigger a "dopamine" signal, activating the O(N) memory evolution pipeline to reshape the agent's knowledge graph. To evaluate performance under realistic conditions, we introduce the LoCoMo-Noise benchmark, which injects controlled conversational noise into long-term sessions. Evaluations demonstrate that D-MEM reduces token consumption by over 80%, eliminates O(N^2) bottlenecks, and outperforms baselines in multi-hop reasoning and adversarial resilience. By selectively gating cognitive restructuring, D-MEM provides a scalable, cost-efficient foundation for lifelong agentic memory.

摘要（中文）: 自主LLM智能体需要结构化的长期记忆，但现有的“追加-演化”系统，如A-MEM，面临O(N^2)的写入延迟和过高的Token成本。我们引入D-MEM（多巴胺门控智能体记忆），一种受生物学启发的架构，它通过基于奖励预测误差(RPE)的快/慢路由系统，将短期交互与认知重构解耦。一个轻量级的评论家路由评估刺激的惊奇度和效用。常规的、低RPE的输入被绕过或缓存在O(1)快速访问缓冲区中。相反，高RPE的输入，如事实矛盾或偏好转变，会触发一个“多巴胺”信号，激活O(N)的记忆演化流水线，以重塑智能体的知识图谱。为了在实际条件下评估性能，我们引入了LoCoMo-Noise基准，它将受控的对话噪声注入到长期会话中。评估表明，D-MEM将Token消耗降低了80%以上，消除了O(N^2)的瓶颈，并在多跳推理和对抗性鲁棒性方面优于基线。通过选择性地门控认知重构，D-MEM为终身智能体记忆提供了一个可扩展、经济高效的基础。

TLDR: D-MEM introduces a biologically inspired memory architecture for LLM agents that uses reward prediction error (RPE) to selectively update long-term memory, reducing token consumption and improving performance in long-term reasoning and adversarial resilience.

简要说明: D-MEM提出了一种受生物学启发的LLM Agent记忆架构，该架构使用奖励预测误差（RPE）选择性地更新长期记忆，从而减少token消耗并提高长期推理和鲁棒性。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (9/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yuru Song, Qi Xin

Look Before Acting: Enhancing Vision Foundation Representations for Vision-Language-Action Models

Abstract (English): Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have recently emerged as a promising paradigm for robotic manipulation, in which reliable action prediction critically depends on accurately interpreting and integrating visual observations conditioned on language instructions. Although recent works have sought to enhance the visual capabilities of VLA models, most approaches treat the LLM backbone as a black box, providing limited insight into how visual information is grounded into action generation. Therefore, we perform a systematic analysis of multiple VLA models across different action-generation paradigms and observe that sensitivity to visual tokens progressively decreases in deeper layers during action generation. Motivated by this observation, we propose \textbf{DeepVision-VLA}, built on a \textbf{Vision-Language Mixture-of-Transformers (VL-MoT)} framework. This framework enables shared attention between the vision foundation model and the VLA backbone, injecting multi-level visual features from the vision expert into deeper layers of the VLA backbone to enhance visual representations for precise and complex manipulation. In addition, we introduce \textbf{Action-Guided Visual Pruning (AGVP)}, which leverages shallow-layer attention to prune irrelevant visual tokens while preserving task-relevant ones, reinforcing critical visual cues for manipulation with minimal computational overhead. DeepVision-VLA outperforms prior state-of-the-art methods by 9.0\% and 7.5\% on simulated and real-world tasks, respectively, providing new insights for the design of visually enhanced VLA models.

摘要（中文）: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型最近作为一种有前景的机器人操作范式出现，其中可靠的动作预测关键取决于准确地解释和整合以语言指令为条件的视觉观察。尽管最近的研究试图增强 VLA 模型的视觉能力，但大多数方法将 LLM 主干视为黑盒，对视觉信息如何融入到动作生成中提供的 insights 有限。因此，我们对跨越不同动作生成范式的多个 VLA 模型进行了系统分析，并观察到在动作生成过程中，对视觉 token 的敏感度在更深的层中逐渐降低。受此观察的启发，我们提出了 \textbf{DeepVision-VLA}，它建立在一个 \textbf{视觉-语言混合 Transformer (VL-MoT)} 框架之上。该框架实现了视觉基础模型和 VLA 主干之间的共享注意力，并将来自视觉专家的多层视觉特征注入到 VLA 主干的更深层中，以增强视觉表示，从而实现精确而复杂的操作。此外，我们引入了 \textbf{动作引导的视觉剪枝 (AGVP)}，它利用浅层注意力来剪除不相关的视觉 token，同时保留与任务相关的 token，从而以最小的计算开销强化用于操作的关键视觉线索。DeepVision-VLA 在模拟和真实世界的任务中分别优于先前的最先进方法 9.0% 和 7.5%，为视觉增强的 VLA 模型的设计提供了新的见解。

TLDR: This paper introduces DeepVision-VLA, a Vision-Language-Action model that enhances visual representations by injecting multi-level visual features into deeper layers and pruning irrelevant visual tokens, achieving state-of-the-art performance in robotic manipulation.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了 DeepVision-VLA，一种视觉-语言-动作模型，通过将多层视觉特征注入到更深层并修剪不相关的视觉标记来增强视觉表示，从而在机器人操作中实现了最先进的性能。

Relevance: (10/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yulin Luo, Hao Chen, Zhuangzhe Wu, Bowen Sui, Jiaming Liu, Chenyang Gu, Zhuoyang Liu, Qiuxuan Feng, Jiale Yu, Shuo Gu, Peng Jia, Pheng-Ann Heng, Shanghang Zhang

Trajectory-Diversity-Driven Robust Vision-and-Language Navigation

Abstract (English): Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN) requires agents to navigate photo-realistic environments following natural language instructions. Current methods predominantly rely on imitation learning, which suffers from limited generalization and poor robustness to execution perturbations. We present NavGRPO, a reinforcement learning framework that learns goal-directed navigation policies through Group Relative Policy Optimization. By exploring diverse trajectories and optimizing via within-group performance comparisons, our method enables agents to distinguish effective strategies beyond expert paths without requiring additional value networks. Built on ScaleVLN, NavGRPO achieves superior robustness on R2R and REVERIE benchmarks with +3.0% and +1.71% SPL improvements in unseen environments. Under extreme early-stage perturbations, we demonstrate +14.89% SPL gain over the baseline, confirming that goal-directed RL training builds substantially more robust navigation policies. Code and models will be released.

摘要（中文）: 视觉-语言导航（VLN）要求智能体根据自然语言指令在逼真的照片环境中进行导航。当前的方法主要依赖于模仿学习，这存在泛化能力有限以及对执行扰动鲁棒性差的问题。我们提出 NavGRPO，一种强化学习框架，它通过群组相对策略优化来学习以目标为导向的导航策略。 通过探索多样化的轨迹并通过群组内的性能比较进行优化，我们的方法使智能体能够区分专家路径之外的有效策略，而无需额外的价值网络。 NavGRPO 构建于 ScaleVLN 之上，在 R2R 和 REVERIE 基准测试中获得了卓越的鲁棒性，在未见环境中 SPL 分别提高了 +3.0% 和 +1.71%。 在极端的早期阶段扰动下，我们的 SPL 增益超过基线 +14.89%，证实了以目标为导向的强化学习训练构建了更加鲁棒的导航策略。 代码和模型将开源。

TLDR: The paper introduces NavGRPO, a reinforcement learning framework for Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN) that uses group relative policy optimization to achieve robustness against execution perturbations, demonstrating improved performance on R2R and REVERIE benchmarks.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种用于视觉语言导航的强化学习框架NavGRPO，它使用组相对策略优化来实现对执行扰动的鲁棒性，并在R2R和REVERIE基准测试中表现出改进的性能。

Relevance: (10/10)
Novelty: (8/10)

Authors: Jiangyang Li, Cong Wan, SongLin Dong, Chenhao Ding, Qiang Wang, Zhiheng Ma, Yihong Gong

RealVLG-R1: A Large-Scale Real-World Visual-Language Grounding Benchmark for Robotic Perception and Manipulation

Abstract (English): Visual-language grounding aims to establish semantic correspondences between natural language and visual entities, enabling models to accurately identify and localize target objects based on textual instructions. Existing VLG approaches focus on coarse-grained, object-level localization, while traditional robotic grasping methods rely predominantly on geometric cues and lack language guidance, which limits their applicability in language-driven manipulation scenarios. To address these limitations, we propose the RealVLG framework, which integrates the RealVLG-11B dataset and the RealVLG-R1 model to unify real-world visual-language grounding and grasping tasks. RealVLG-11B dataset provides multi-granularity annotations including bounding boxes, segmentation masks, grasp poses, contact points, and human-verified fine-grained language descriptions, covering approximately 165,000 images, over 800 object instances, 1.3 million segmentation, detection, and language annotations, and roughly 11 billion grasping examples. Building on this dataset, RealVLG-R1 employs Reinforcement Fine-tuning on pretrained large-scale vision-language models to predict bounding boxes, segmentation masks, grasp poses, and contact points in a unified manner given natural language instructions. Experimental results demonstrate that RealVLG supports zero-shot perception and manipulation in real-world unseen environments, establishing a unified semantic-visual multimodal benchmark that provides a comprehensive data and evaluation platform for language-driven robotic perception and grasping policy learning. All data and code are publicly available at https://github.com/lif314/RealVLG-R1.

摘要（中文）: 视觉-语言对齐旨在建立自然语言和视觉实体之间的语义对应关系，使模型能够根据文本指令准确地识别和定位目标对象。 现有的视觉-语言对齐方法主要关注粗粒度的对象级定位，而传统的机器人抓取方法主要依赖于几何线索，缺乏语言引导，这限制了它们在语言驱动操作场景中的适用性。 为解决这些局限性，我们提出了RealVLG框架，该框架集成了RealVLG-11B数据集和RealVLG-R1模型，以统一现实世界的视觉-语言对齐和抓取任务。 RealVLG-11B数据集提供多粒度标注，包括边界框、分割掩码、抓取姿态、接触点和人工验证的细粒度语言描述，涵盖约165,000张图像、超过800个对象实例、130万个分割、检测和语言标注，以及约110亿个抓取示例。 在此数据集之上，RealVLG-R1采用对预训练的大规模视觉-语言模型进行强化微调的方法，以统一的方式预测自然语言指令下的边界框、分割掩码、抓取姿态和接触点。 实验结果表明，RealVLG支持真实世界未见环境中的零样本感知和操作，从而建立了一个统一的语义-视觉多模态基准，为语言驱动的机器人感知和抓取策略学习提供了全面的数据和评估平台。 所有数据和代码均可在 https://github.com/lif314/RealVLG-R1 上公开获取。

TLDR: The paper introduces RealVLG-R1, a large-scale real-world visual-language grounding benchmark for robotic perception and manipulation, featuring a dataset (RealVLG-11B) and a model (RealVLG-R1) for unified grounding and grasping based on language instructions.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了RealVLG-R1，一个大规模的真实世界视觉-语言接地的基准测试，用于机器人感知和操作。它包含一个数据集（RealVLG-11B）和一个模型（RealVLG-R1），用于基于语言指令的统一接地和抓取。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)

Authors: Linfei Li, Lin Zhang, Ying Shen

HSImul3R: Physics-in-the-Loop Reconstruction of Simulation-Ready Human-Scene Interactions

Abstract (English): We present HSImul3R, a unified framework for simulation-ready 3D reconstruction of human-scene interactions (HSI) from casual captures, including sparse-view images and monocular videos. Existing methods suffer from a perception-simulation gap: visually plausible reconstructions often violate physical constraints, leading to instability in physics engines and failure in embodied AI applications. To bridge this gap, we introduce a physically-grounded bi-directional optimization pipeline that treats the physics simulator as an active supervisor to jointly refine human dynamics and scene geometry. In the forward direction, we employ Scene-targeted Reinforcement Learning to optimize human motion under dual supervision of motion fidelity and contact stability. In the reverse direction, we propose Direct Simulation Reward Optimization, which leverages simulation feedback on gravitational stability and interaction success to refine scene geometry. We further present HSIBench, a new benchmark with diverse objects and interaction scenarios. Extensive experiments demonstrate that HSImul3R produces the first stable, simulation-ready HSI reconstructions and can be directly deployed to real-world humanoid robots.

摘要（中文）: 我们提出了HSImul3R，一个统一的框架，用于从随意拍摄的图像（包括稀疏视角的图像和单目视频）中进行可用于仿真的三维人类-场景交互（HSI）重建。现有方法受限于感知-仿真差距：视觉上合理的重建通常违反物理约束，导致物理引擎中的不稳定性和具身人工智能应用中的失败。为了弥合这一差距，我们引入了一种物理驱动的双向优化流程，该流程将物理模拟器视为一个主动监督器，以联合优化人类动力学和场景几何。在正向方向，我们采用以场景为目标的强化学习，在运动保真度和接触稳定性的双重监督下，优化人类运动。在反向方向，我们提出了直接仿真奖励优化，该方法利用重力稳定性以及交互成功方面的仿真反馈来优化场景几何。我们进一步提出了HSIBench，一个新的基准，包含多种多样的物体和交互场景。大量的实验证明，HSImul3R 产生首批稳定、可用于仿真的 HSI 重建结果，并且可以直接部署到现实世界的人形机器人中。

TLDR: HSImul3R presents a physics-in-the-loop framework for reconstructing simulation-ready human-scene interactions from casual captures, addressing the perception-simulation gap with a physically-grounded optimization pipeline and a new benchmark called HSIBench.

简要说明: HSImul3R 提出了一个物理在环框架，用于从偶然拍摄中重建可用于仿真的场景中人与人的互动，通过物理基础的优化管道和一个名为 HSIBench 的新基准来解决感知-仿真差距。

Authors: Yukang Cao, Haozhe Xie, Fangzhou Hong, Long Zhuo, Zhaoxi Chen, Liang Pan, Ziwei Liu

Panoramic Affordance Prediction

Abstract (English): Affordance prediction serves as a critical bridge between perception and action in embodied AI. However, existing research is confined to pinhole camera models, which suffer from narrow Fields of View (FoV) and fragmented observations, often missing critical holistic environmental context. In this paper, we present the first exploration into Panoramic Affordance Prediction, utilizing 360-degree imagery to capture global spatial relationships and holistic scene understanding. To facilitate this novel task, we first introduce PAP-12K, a large-scale benchmark dataset containing over 1,000 ultra-high-resolution (12k, 11904 x 5952) panoramic images with over 12k carefully annotated QA pairs and affordance masks. Furthermore, we propose PAP, a training-free, coarse-to-fine pipeline inspired by the human foveal visual system to tackle the ultra-high resolution and severe distortion inherent in panoramic images. PAP employs recursive visual routing via grid prompting to progressively locate targets, applies an adaptive gaze mechanism to rectify local geometric distortions, and utilizes a cascaded grounding pipeline to extract precise instance-level masks. Experimental results on PAP-12K reveal that existing affordance prediction methods designed for standard perspective images suffer severe performance degradation and fail due to the unique challenges of panoramic vision. In contrast, PAP framework effectively overcomes these obstacles, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art baselines and highlighting the immense potential of panoramic perception for robust embodied intelligence.

摘要（中文）: 具身人工智能中，可供性预测是连接感知与动作的关键桥梁。然而，现有研究局限于针孔相机模型，该模型视野狭窄（FoV）且观测零散，常常遗漏关键的整体环境背景。本文首次探索了全景可供性预测，利用360度图像来捕捉全局空间关系和整体场景理解。为了促进这项新颖任务，我们首先推出了 PAP-12K，这是一个大规模基准数据集，包含超过 1,000 张超高分辨率（12k，11904 x 5952）全景图像，以及超过 12,000 个精心标注的 QA 对和可供性掩码。此外，我们提出 PAP，一个受人类中央凹视觉系统启发的、无需训练的、由粗到精的流水线，以解决全景图像中固有的超高分辨率和严重失真问题。PAP 采用基于网格提示的递归视觉路由来逐步定位目标，应用自适应凝视机制来校正局部几何失真，并利用级联 grounding 流水线来提取精确的实例级掩码。在 PAP-12K 上的实验结果表明，为标准透视图像设计的现有可供性预测方法由于全景视觉的独特挑战而遭受严重的性能下降并失效。相比之下，PAP 框架有效地克服了这些障碍，显著优于最先进的基线方法，并突出了全景感知在实现稳健的具身智能方面的巨大潜力。

TLDR: The paper introduces Panoramic Affordance Prediction (PAP), a new task and benchmark dataset (PAP-12K) for affordance prediction using 360-degree imagery, along with a novel training-free pipeline (PAP) to address the unique challenges of panoramic vision.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了全景可供性预测（PAP），这是一个新的任务和基准数据集（PAP-12K），用于使用360度图像进行可供性预测，以及一个新颖的免训练流水线（PAP），以解决全景视觉的独特挑战。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (9/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Zixin Zhang, Chenfei Liao, Hongfei Zhang, Harold Haodong Chen, Kanghao Chen, Zichen Wen, Litao Guo, Bin Ren, Xu Zheng, Yinchuan Li, Xuming Hu, Nicu Sebe, Ying-Cong Chen

Kimodo: Scaling Controllable Human Motion Generation

Abstract (English): High-quality human motion data is becoming increasingly important for applications in robotics, simulation, and entertainment. Recent generative models offer a potential data source, enabling human motion synthesis through intuitive inputs like text prompts or kinematic constraints on poses. However, the small scale of public mocap datasets has limited the motion quality, control accuracy, and generalization of these models. In this work, we introduce Kimodo, an expressive and controllable kinematic motion diffusion model trained on 700 hours of optical motion capture data. Our model generates high-quality motions while being easily controlled through text and a comprehensive suite of kinematic constraints including full-body keyframes, sparse joint positions/rotations, 2D waypoints, and dense 2D paths. This is enabled through a carefully designed motion representation and two-stage denoiser architecture that decomposes root and body prediction to minimize motion artifacts while allowing for flexible constraint conditioning. Experiments on the large-scale mocap dataset justify key design decisions and analyze how the scaling of dataset size and model size affect performance.

摘要（中文）: 高质量的人体运动数据在机器人、仿真和娱乐应用领域变得越来越重要。近期的生成模型提供了一个潜在的数据来源，能够通过文本提示或姿势的运动学约束等直观输入来合成人体运动。然而，公共动作捕捉数据集规模较小，限制了这些模型的运动质量、控制精度和泛化能力。在本文中，我们推出了Kimodo，一个富有表现力且可控的运动学运动扩散模型，该模型在700小时的光学动作捕捉数据上进行训练。我们的模型生成高质量的运动，同时能够通过文本和一套全面的运动学约束（包括全身关键帧、稀疏关节位置/旋转、2D路标点和密集2D路径）轻松控制。这得益于精心设计的运动表示和两阶段去噪器架构，该架构将根节点和身体预测分解，以最大限度地减少运动伪影，同时允许灵活的约束条件设定。在大规模动作捕捉数据集上的实验验证了关键的设计决策，并分析了数据集规模和模型规模的扩大如何影响性能。

TLDR: Kimodo is a kinematic motion diffusion model trained on a large mocap dataset (700 hours) that allows for high-quality, controllable human motion generation via text and various kinematic constraints.

简要说明: Kimodo是一个运动学运动扩散模型，它在大型运动捕捉数据集（700小时）上训练，允许通过文本和各种运动学约束生成高质量、可控的人体运动。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Davis Rempe, Mathis Petrovich, Ye Yuan, Haotian Zhang, Xue Bin Peng, Yifeng Jiang, Tingwu Wang, Umar Iqbal, David Minor, Michael de Ruyter, Jiefeng Li, Chen Tessler, Edy Lim, Eugene Jeong, Sam Wu, Ehsan Hassani, Michael Huang, Jin-Bey Yu, Chaeyeon Chung, Lina Song, Olivier Dionne, Jan Kautz, Simon Yuen, Sanja Fidler

RoCo Challenge at AAAI 2026: Benchmarking Robotic Collaborative Manipulation for Assembly Towards Industrial Automation

Abstract (English): Embodied Artificial Intelligence (EAI) is rapidly developing, gradually subverting previous autonomous systems' paradigms from isolated perception to integrated, continuous action. This transition is highly significant for industrial robotic manipulation, promising to free human workers from repetitive, dangerous daily labor. To benchmark and advance this capability, we introduce the Robotic Collaborative Assembly Assistance (RoCo) Challenge with a dataset towards simulation and real-world assembly manipulation. Set against the backdrop of human-centered manufacturing, this challenge focuses on a high-precision planetary gearbox assembly task, a demanding yet highly representative operation in modern industry. Built upon a self-developed data collection, training, and evaluation system in Isaac Sim, and utilizing a dual-arm robot for real-world deployment, the challenge operates in two phases. The Simulation Round defines fine-grained task phases for step-wise scoring to handle the long-horizon nature of the assembly. The Real-World Round mirrors this evaluation with physical gearbox components and high-quality teleoperated datasets. The core tasks require assembling an epicyclic gearbox from scratch, including mounting three planet gears, a sun gear, and a ring gear. Attracting over 60 teams and 170+ participants from more than 10 countries, the challenge yielded highly effective solutions, most notably ARC-VLA and RoboCola. Results demonstrate that a dual-model framework for long-horizon multi-task learning is highly effective, and the strategic utilization of recovery-from-failure curriculum data is a critical insight for successful deployment. This report outlines the competition setup, evaluation approach, key findings, and future directions for industrial EAI. Our dataset, CAD files, code, and evaluation results can be found at: https://rocochallenge.github.io/RoCo2026/.

摘要（中文）: 具身人工智能（EAI）正在快速发展，逐渐颠覆以往自主系统从孤立感知到集成、连续行动的范式。这种转变对于工业机器人操作具有重大意义，有望将人类工人从重复、危险的日常劳动中解放出来。为了评估和推进这一能力，我们推出了机器人协同装配辅助（RoCo）挑战赛，并提供数据集，用于模拟和真实世界的装配操作。该挑战赛以人为中心的制造为背景，着重于高精度行星齿轮箱装配任务，这是现代工业中一项要求苛刻且具有高度代表性的操作。该挑战赛建立在Isaac Sim中自主开发的数据收集、训练和评估系统之上，并利用双臂机器人进行真实世界的部署，分为两个阶段进行。模拟轮定义了精细的任务阶段，用于逐步评分，以处理装配的长周期特性。真实世界轮则采用物理齿轮箱组件和高质量遥操作数据集来反映这种评估。核心任务需要从头开始装配行星齿轮箱，包括安装三个行星齿轮、一个太阳齿轮和一个齿圈。本次挑战赛吸引了来自10多个国家的60多个团队和170多名参与者，产生了高效的解决方案，其中最值得注意的是ARC-VLA和RoboCola。结果表明，用于长周期多任务学习的双模型框架非常有效，并且策略性地利用从失败中恢复的课程数据是成功部署的关键见解。本报告概述了竞赛设置、评估方法、主要发现以及工业EAI的未来方向。我们的数据集、CAD文件、代码和评估结果可在以下网址找到：https://rocochallenge.github.io/RoCo2026/。

TLDR: The RoCo Challenge at AAAI 2026 focuses on benchmarking robotic collaborative assembly manipulation for industrial automation, using a dual-arm robot and a realistic epicyclic gearbox assembly task in simulation and the real world, revealing effective solutions for long-horizon multi-task learning and recovery from failure.

简要说明: AAAI 2026 的 RoCo 挑战赛旨在评估机器人协作装配操作在工业自动化中的应用，在模拟和现实世界中使用双臂机器人和现实的行星齿轮箱装配任务，揭示了长时期多任务学习和从故障中恢复的有效解决方案。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Haichao Liu, Yuheng Zhou, Zhenyu Wu, Ziheng Ji, Ziyu Shan, Qianzhun Wang, Ruixuan Liu, Zhiyuan Yang, Yejun Gu, Shalman Khan, Shijun Yan, Jun Liu, Haiyue Zhu, Changliu Liu, Jianfei Yang, Jingbing Zhang, Ziwei Wang

Zero-Shot Generalization from Motion Demonstrations to New Tasks

Abstract (English): Learning motion policies from expert demonstrations is an essential paradigm in modern robotics. While end-to-end models aim for broad generalization, they require large datasets and computationally heavy inference. Conversely, learning dynamical systems (DS) provides fast, reactive, and provably stable control from very few demonstrations. However, existing DS learning methods typically model isolated tasks and struggle to reuse demonstrations for novel behaviors. In this work, we formalize the problem of combining isolated demonstrations within a shared workspace to enable generalization to unseen tasks. The Gaussian Graph is introduced, which reinterprets spatial components of learned motion primitives as discrete vertices with connections to one another. This formulation allows us to bridge continuous control with discrete graph search. We propose two frameworks leveraging this graph: Stitching, for constructing time-invariant DSs, and Chaining, giving a sequence-based DS for complex motions while retaining convergence guarantees. Simulations and real-robot experiments show that these methods successfully generalize to new tasks where baseline methods fail.

摘要（中文）: 从专家演示中学习运动策略是现代机器人技术中的一种重要范式。尽管端到端模型旨在实现广泛的泛化，但它们需要大型数据集和计算量大的推理。相反，学习动力系统（DS）能够从极少的演示中提供快速、反应灵敏且可证明稳定的控制。然而，现有的DS学习方法通常对孤立的任务进行建模，难以重用演示来完成新的行为。在这项工作中，我们将共享工作空间内组合孤立演示的问题形式化，以实现对未见任务的泛化。我们引入了高斯图，它将学习到的运动基元的空间分量重新解释为离散顶点，彼此之间存在连接。这种形式化允许我们将连续控制与离散图搜索连接起来。我们提出了利用此图的两个框架：拼接（Stitching），用于构建时不变DS；链式连接（Chaining），提供基于序列的DS用于复杂运动，同时保留收敛保证。仿真和真实机器人实验表明，这些方法成功地泛化到基线方法失败的新任务。

TLDR: This paper introduces a method for generalizing motion policies learned from demonstrations to new robotic tasks by representing motion primitives as a Gaussian Graph, enabling both time-invariant and sequence-based dynamical systems for control.

简要说明: 本文介绍了一种将从演示中学习到的运动策略推广到新的机器人任务的方法，通过将运动原语表示为高斯图，从而实现用于控制的时不变和基于序列的动力系统。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Kilian Freitag, Alvin Combrink, Nadia Figueroa

Efficient Morphology-Control Co-Design via Stackelberg Proximal Policy Optimization

Abstract (English): Morphology-control co-design concerns the coupled optimization of an agent's body structure and control policy. This problem exhibits a bi-level structure, where the control dynamically adapts to the morphology to maximize performance. Existing methods typically neglect the control's adaptation dynamics by adopting a single-level formulation that treats the control policy as fixed when optimizing morphology. This can lead to inefficient optimization, as morphology updates may be misaligned with control adaptation. In this paper, we revisit the co-design problem from a game-theoretic perspective, modeling the intrinsic coupling between morphology and control as a novel variant of a Stackelberg game. We propose Stackelberg Proximal Policy Optimization (Stackelberg PPO), which explicitly incorporates the control's adaptation dynamics into morphology optimization. By modeling this intrinsic coupling, our method aligns morphology updates with control adaptation, thereby stabilizing training and improving learning efficiency. Experiments across diverse co-design tasks demonstrate that Stackelberg PPO outperforms standard PPO in both stability and final performance, opening the way for dramatically more efficient robotics designs.

摘要（中文）: 形态控制协同设计关注智能体身体结构和控制策略的耦合优化。该问题呈现双层结构，其中控制动态适应形态以最大化性能。现有方法通常忽略控制的适应动态，而是采用单层公式，在优化形态时将控制策略视为固定不变。这可能导致效率低下，因为形态更新可能与控制适应不一致。在本文中，我们从博弈论的角度重新审视协同设计问题，将形态和控制之间的内在耦合建模为 Stackelberg 博弈的一种新变体。我们提出了 Stackelberg 近端策略优化(Stackelberg PPO)，它将控制的适应动态显式地融入到形态优化中。通过对这种内在耦合进行建模，我们的方法使形态更新与控制适应对齐，从而稳定训练并提高学习效率。在各种协同设计任务上的实验表明，Stackelberg PPO 在稳定性和最终性能方面均优于标准 PPO，为更高效的机器人设计开辟了道路。

TLDR: This paper introduces Stackelberg Proximal Policy Optimization (Stackelberg PPO) for efficient morphology-control co-design in robotics, addressing the limitations of existing methods by explicitly modeling the adaptation dynamics between morphology and control.

简要说明: 本文介绍了Stackelberg近端策略优化(Stackelberg PPO)方法，用于机器人中高效的形态-控制协同设计，通过显式地建模形态和控制之间的自适应动态，解决了现有方法的局限性。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)

Authors: Yanning Dai, Yuhui Wang, Dylan R. Ashley, Jürgen Schmidhuber

MoE-ACT: Scaling Multi-Task Bimanual Manipulation with Sparse Language-Conditioned Mixture-of-Experts Transformers

Abstract (English): The ability of robots to handle multiple tasks under a unified policy is critical for deploying embodied intelligence in real-world household and industrial applications. However, out-of-distribution variation across tasks often causes severe task interference and negative transfer when training general robotic policies. To address this challenge, we propose a lightweight multi-task imitation learning framework for bimanual manipulation, termed Mixture-of-Experts-Enhanced Action Chunking Transformer (MoE-ACT), which integrates sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) modules into the Transformer encoder of ACT. The MoE layer decomposes a unified task policy into independently invoked expert components. Through adaptive activation, it naturally decouples multi-task action distributions in latent space. During decoding, Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM) dynamically modulates action tokens to improve consistency between action generation and task instructions. In parallel, multi-scale cross-attention enables the policy to simultaneously focus on both low-level and high-level semantic features, providing rich visual information for robotic manipulation. We further incorporate textual information, transitioning the framework from a purely vision-based model to a vision-centric, language-conditioned action generation system. Experimental validation in both simulation and a real-world dual-arm setup shows that MoE-ACT substantially improves multi-task performance. Specifically, MoE-ACT outperforms vanilla ACT by an average of 33% in success rate. These results indicate that MoE-ACT provides stronger robustness and generalization in complex multi-task bimanual manipulation environments. Our open-source project page can be found at https://j3k7.github.io/MoE-ACT/.

摘要（中文）: 机器人执行统一策略下的多项任务的能力对于在现实世界的家庭和工业应用中部署具身智能至关重要。然而，跨任务的分布外变化通常会导致训练通用机器人策略时出现严重的任务干扰和负迁移。为了解决这一挑战，我们提出了一种轻量级的双臂操作多任务模仿学习框架，称为专家混合增强动作分块Transformer (MoE-ACT)，它将稀疏专家混合(MoE)模块集成到ACT的Transformer编码器中。MoE层将统一任务策略分解为独立调用的专家组件。通过自适应激活，它自然地解耦了潜在空间中的多任务动作分布。在解码过程中，特征线性调制(FiLM)动态地调节动作令牌，以提高动作生成和任务指令之间的一致性。同时，多尺度交叉注意力使策略能够同时关注低级和高级语义特征，为机器人操作提供丰富的视觉信息。我们进一步整合了文本信息，将框架从纯粹的基于视觉的模型过渡到以视觉为中心、语言条件化的动作生成系统。在仿真和真实世界的双臂设置中的实验验证表明，MoE-ACT显著提高了多任务性能。具体来说，MoE-ACT的成功率平均比原始ACT高出33%。这些结果表明，MoE-ACT在复杂的多任务双臂操作环境中提供了更强的鲁棒性和泛化性。我们的开源项目页面可以在https://j3k7.github.io/MoE-ACT/ 找到。

TLDR: The paper introduces MoE-ACT, a multi-task imitation learning framework for bimanual manipulation that uses Mixture-of-Experts and Transformers to improve performance and generalization in complex robotic tasks, showing a 33% improvement over vanilla ACT.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了MoE-ACT，一种用于双臂操作的多任务模仿学习框架，它使用混合专家和Transformer来提高复杂机器人任务中的性能和泛化能力，与普通ACT相比，成功率提高了33%。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Authors: Kangjun Guo, Haichao Liu, Yanji Sun, Ruhan Zhao, Jinni Zhou, Jun Ma

HapticVLA: Contact-Rich Manipulation via Vision-Language-Action Model without Inference-Time Tactile Sensing

Abstract (English): Tactile sensing is a crucial capability for Vision-Language-Action (VLA) architectures, as it enables dexterous and safe manipulation in contact-rich tasks. However, reliance on dedicated tactile hardware increases cost and reduces reproducibility across robotic platforms. We argue that tactile-aware manipulation can be learned offline and deployed without direct haptic feedback at inference. To this end, we present HapticVLA, which proceeds in two tightly coupled stages: Safety-Aware Reward-Weighted Flow Matching (SA-RWFM) and Tactile Distillation (TD). SA-RWFM trains a flow-matching action expert that incorporates precomputed, safety-aware tactile rewards penalizing excessive grasping force and suboptimal grasping trajectories. TD further transfers this tactile-aware capability into a conventional VLA: we distill a compact tactile token from the SA-RWFM teacher and train a student VLA to predict that token from vision and state modalities, enabling tactile-aware action generation at inference without requiring on-board tactile sensors. This design preserves contact-rich tactile-aware reasoning within VLA while removing the need for on-board tactile sensors during deployment. On real-world experiments, HapticVLA achieves a mean success rate of 86.7%, consistently outperforming baseline VLAs - including versions provided with direct tactile feedback during inference.

摘要（中文）: 触觉感知是视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 架构的关键能力，因为它能够实现富接触任务中的灵巧和安全操作。然而，对专用触觉硬件的依赖会增加成本并降低机器人平台之间的可重复性。我们认为，触觉感知的操作可以在离线学习，并在推理时无需直接触觉反馈即可部署。为此，我们提出了 HapticVLA，它包含两个紧密耦合的阶段：安全感知奖励加权流匹配 (SA-RWFM) 和触觉蒸馏 (TD)。SA-RWFM 训练一个流匹配动作专家，该专家整合了预先计算的、安全感知的触觉奖励，以惩罚过度的抓取力和次优的抓取轨迹。TD 进一步将这种触觉感知能力转移到传统的 VLA 中：我们从 SA-RWFM 教师中提炼出一个紧凑的触觉标记，并训练一个学生 VLA 从视觉和状态模态预测该标记，从而在推理时实现触觉感知的动作生成，而无需板载触觉传感器。这种设计保留了 VLA 中富接触的触觉感知推理，同时消除了部署期间对板载触觉传感器的需求。在真实世界的实验中，HapticVLA 实现了 86.7% 的平均成功率，始终优于基线 VLA，包括在推理期间提供直接触觉反馈的版本。

TLDR: HapticVLA enables contact-rich robot manipulation by training a VLA model offline using tactile rewards and distilling tactile knowledge into a sensor-free student VLA, achieving high success rates in real-world tasks.

简要说明: HapticVLA通过离线训练VLA模型，利用触觉奖励并将触觉知识提炼到无传感器的学生VLA中，实现了接触丰富的机器人操作，并在真实世界任务中取得了很高的成功率。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Konstantin Gubernatorov, Mikhail Sannikov, Ilya Mikhalchuk, Egor Kuznetsov, Makar Artemov, Ogunwoye Faith Ouwatobi, Marcelino Fernando, Artem Asanov, Ziang Guo, Dzmitry Tsetserukou

NavGSim: High-Fidelity Gaussian Splatting Simulator for Large-Scale Navigation

Abstract (English): Simulating realistic environments for robots is widely recognized as a critical challenge in robot learning, particularly in terms of rendering and physical simulation. This challenge becomes even more pronounced in navigation tasks, where trajectories often extend across multiple rooms or entire floors. In this work, we present NavGSim, a Gaussian Splatting-based simulator designed to generate high-fidelity, large-scale navigation environments. Built upon a hierarchical 3D Gaussian Splatting framework, NavGSim enables photorealistic rendering in expansive scenes spanning hundreds of square meters. To simulate navigation collisions, we introduce a Gaussian Splatting-based slice technique that directly extracts navigable areas from reconstructed Gaussians. Additionally, for ease of use, we provide comprehensive NavGSim APIs supporting multi-GPU development, including tools for custom scene reconstruction, robot configuration, policy training, and evaluation. To evaluate NavGSim's effectiveness, we train a Vision-Language-Action (VLA) model using trajectories collected from NavGSim and assess its performance in both simulated and real-world environments. Our results demonstrate that NavGSim significantly enhances the VLA model's scene understanding, enabling the policy to handle diverse navigation queries effectively.

摘要（中文）: 为机器人模拟逼真环境是机器人学习中公认的关键挑战，尤其是在渲染和物理模拟方面。在导航任务中，这一挑战更为突出，因为轨迹通常会跨越多个房间或整个楼层。 本文提出NavGSim，一种基于高斯溅射的模拟器，旨在生成高保真、大规模的导航环境。 NavGSim构建于分层3D高斯溅射框架之上，能够在跨越数百平方米的广阔场景中实现照片级逼真的渲染。为了模拟导航碰撞，我们引入了一种基于高斯溅射的切片技术，该技术能够直接从重建的高斯分布中提取可导航区域。 此外，为了方便使用，我们提供了全面的NavGSim API，支持多GPU开发，包括用于自定义场景重建、机器人配置、策略训练和评估的工具。 为了评估NavGSim的有效性，我们使用从NavGSim收集的轨迹训练了一个视觉-语言-动作(VLA)模型，并评估了其在模拟和真实环境中的性能。 我们的结果表明，NavGSim显著增强了VLA模型的场景理解能力，使策略能够有效地处理各种导航查询。

TLDR: The paper introduces NavGSim, a Gaussian Splatting-based simulator for generating high-fidelity, large-scale navigation environments, demonstrating its effectiveness by training a Vision-Language-Action model.

简要说明: 本文介绍NavGSim，一个基于高斯溅射的模拟器，用于生成高保真、大规模的导航环境，并通过训练视觉-语言-动作模型证明了其有效性。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Jiahang Liu, Yuanxing Duan, Jiazhao Zhang, Minghan Li, Shaoan Wang, Zhizheng Zhang, He Wang

What Matters for Scalable and Robust Learning in End-to-End Driving Planners?

Abstract (English): End-to-end autonomous driving has gained significant attention for its potential to learn robust behavior in interactive scenarios and scale with data. Popular architectures often build on separate modules for perception and planning connected through latent representations, such as bird's eye view feature grids, to maintain end-to-end differentiability. This paradigm emerged mostly on open-loop datasets, with evaluation focusing not only on driving performance, but also intermediate perception tasks. Unfortunately, architectural advances that excel in open-loop often fail to translate to scalable learning of robust closed-loop driving. In this paper, we systematically re-examine the impact of common architectural patterns on closed-loop performance: (1) high-resolution perceptual representations, (2) disentangled trajectory representations, and (3) generative planning. Crucially, our analysis evaluates the combined impact of these patterns, revealing both unexpected limitations as well as underexplored synergies. Building on these insights, we introduce BevAD, a novel lightweight and highly scalable end-to-end driving architecture. BevAD achieves 72.7% success rate on the Bench2Drive benchmark and demonstrates strong data-scaling behavior using pure imitation learning. Our code and models are publicly available here: https://dmholtz.github.io/bevad/

摘要（中文）: 端到端自动驾驶因其在交互式场景中学习稳健行为和利用数据进行扩展的潜力而备受关注。流行的架构通常建立在独立的感知和规划模块之上，并通过潜在表示（如鸟瞰图特征网格）连接，以保持端到端可微性。这种范式主要出现在开环数据集上，评估不仅关注驾驶性能，还关注中间感知任务。然而，在开环中表现出色的架构进展往往无法转化为稳健闭环驾驶的可扩展学习。在本文中，我们系统地重新审视了常见架构模式对闭环性能的影响：(1) 高分辨率感知表示，(2) 解耦的轨迹表示，以及 (3) 生成式规划。至关重要的是，我们的分析评估了这些模式的综合影响，揭示了意想不到的局限性以及未充分探索的协同效应。基于这些见解，我们引入了BevAD，一种新型轻量级且高度可扩展的端到端驾驶架构。BevAD在Bench2Drive基准测试中取得了72.7%的成功率，并展示了使用纯模仿学习的强大数据扩展行为。我们的代码和模型在此处公开提供：https://dmholtz.github.io/bevad/

TLDR: This paper investigates the efficacy of different architectural choices in end-to-end autonomous driving planners, identifying limitations and synergies. It introduces BevAD, a novel architecture achieving state-of-the-art performance on a driving benchmark.

简要说明: 本文研究了端到端自动驾驶规划器中不同架构选择的有效性，识别了局限性和协同效应。 它介绍了一种名为BevAD的新型架构，该架构在驾驶基准测试中实现了最先进的性能。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: David Holtz, Niklas Hanselmann, Simon Doll, Marius Cordts, Bernt Schiele

KiRAS: Keyframe Guided Self-Imitation for Robust and Adaptive Skill Learning in Quadruped Robots

Abstract (English): With advances in reinforcement learning and imitation learning, quadruped robots can acquire diverse skills within a single policy by imitating multiple skill-specific datasets. However, the lack of datasets on complex terrains limits the ability of such multi-skill policies to generalize effectively in unstructured environments. Inspired by animation, we adopt keyframes as minimal and universal skill representations, relaxing dataset constraints and enabling the integration of terrain adaptability with skill diversity. We propose Keyframe Guided Self-Imitation for Robust and Adaptive Skill Learning (KiRAS), an end-to-end framework for acquiring and transitioning between diverse skill primitives on complex terrains. KiRAS first learns diverse skills on flat terrain through keyframe-guided self-imitation, eliminating the need for expert datasets; then continues training the same policy network on rough terrains to enhance robustness. To eliminate catastrophic forgetting, a proficiency-based Skill Initialization Technique is introduced. Experiments on Solo-8 and Unitree Go1 robots show that KiRAS enables robust skill acquisition and smooth transitions across challenging terrains. This framework demonstrates its potential as a lightweight platform for multi-skill generation and dataset collection. It further enables flexible skill transitions that enhance locomotion on challenging terrains.

摘要（中文）: 随着强化学习和模仿学习的进步，四足机器人可以通过模仿多个特定技能的数据集，在单个策略中获得多种技能。然而，缺乏复杂地形的数据集限制了这种多技能策略在非结构化环境中有效泛化的能力。受动画的启发，我们采用关键帧作为最小且通用的技能表示，放宽了数据集的约束，并实现了地形适应性与技能多样性的集成。我们提出了一种名为关键帧引导的自模仿用于稳健和适应性技能学习（KiRAS）的端到端框架，用于在复杂地形上获取并过渡不同技能基元。KiRAS首先通过关键帧引导的自模仿学习在平坦地形上的多种技能，消除了对专家数据集的需求； 然后在粗糙地形上继续训练相同的策略网络，以增强稳健性。为了消除灾难性遗忘，引入了一种基于熟练度的技能初始化技术。在Solo-8和Unitree Go1机器人上的实验表明，KiRAS能够实现稳健的技能获取和在具有挑战性的地形上的平滑过渡。该框架展示了其作为多技能生成和数据集收集的轻量级平台的潜力。它进一步实现了灵活的技能过渡，从而增强了在具有挑战性的地形上的运动能力。

TLDR: The paper introduces KiRAS, a keyframe-guided self-imitation framework for quadruped robots to learn robust and adaptive skills on complex terrains without expert datasets, using a two-stage training approach and a skill initialization technique.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了KiRAS，一种基于关键帧引导的自模仿框架，用于四足机器人学习在复杂地形上稳健且适应性强的技能，无需专家数据集，采用两阶段训练方法和技能初始化技术。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Xiaoyi Wei, Peng Zhai, Jiaxin Tu, Yueqi Zhang, Yuqi Li, Zonghao Zhang, Hu Zhou, Lihua Zhang

ForceVLA2: Unleashing Hybrid Force-Position Control with Force Awareness for Contact-Rich Manipulation

Abstract (English): Embodied intelligence for contact-rich manipulation has predominantly relied on position control, while explicit awareness and regulation of interaction forces remain under-explored, limiting stability, precision, and robustness in real-world tasks. We propose ForceVLA2, an end-to-end vision-language-action framework that equips robots with hybrid force-position control and explicit force awareness. ForceVLA2 introduces force-based prompts into the VLM expert to construct force-aware task concepts across stages, and employs a Cross-Scale Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) in the action expert to adaptively fuse these concepts with real-time interaction forces for closed-loop hybrid force-position regulation. To support learning and evaluation, we construct ForceVLA2-Dataset, containing 1,000 trajectories over 5 contact-rich tasks, including wiping, pressing, and assembling, with multi-view images, task prompts, proprioceptive state, and force signals. Extensive experiments show that ForceVLA2 substantially improves success rates and reliability in contact-rich manipulation, outperforming pi0 and pi0.5 by 48.0% and 35.0%, respectively, across the 5 tasks, and mitigating common failure modes such as arm overload and unstable contact, thereby actively advancing force-aware interactive physical intelligence in VLAs. The project page is available at https://sites.google.com/view/force-vla2/home.

摘要（中文）: 用于接触密集型操作的具身智能主要依赖于位置控制，而对交互力的显式感知和调节仍未得到充分探索，这限制了在现实世界任务中的稳定性、精度和鲁棒性。我们提出了ForceVLA2，一个端到端的视觉-语言-动作框架，它赋予机器人混合力-位置控制和显式力感知能力。ForceVLA2将基于力的提示引入VLM专家，以构建跨阶段的力感知任务概念，并在动作专家中使用跨尺度混合专家（MoE）模型，用于自适应地将这些概念与实时交互力融合，以实现闭环混合力-位置调节。为了支持学习和评估，我们构建了ForceVLA2-数据集，其中包含涵盖5个接触密集型任务（包括擦拭、按压和组装）的1,000条轨迹，数据包括多视角图像、任务提示、本体感受状态和力信号。大量实验表明，ForceVLA2显著提高了接触密集型操作的成功率和可靠性，在5个任务中分别优于pi0和pi0.5 48.0%和35.0%，并减轻了如手臂过载和不稳定接触等常见故障模式，从而积极推进了VLA中力感知的交互式物理智能。项目主页见https://sites.google.com/view/force-vla2/home。

TLDR: The paper introduces ForceVLA2, a vision-language-action framework for robots, enabling hybrid force-position control via force-aware prompts and a cross-scale mixture-of-experts, and demonstrates improved performance on contact-rich manipulation tasks.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了ForceVLA2，一个用于机器人的视觉-语言-动作框架，通过力感知的提示和跨尺度混合专家实现混合力-位置控制，并在接触丰富的操作任务中展示了改进的性能。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yang Li, Zhaxizhuoma, Hongru Jiang, Junjie Xia, Hongquan Zhang, Jinda Du, Yunsong Zhou, Jia Zeng, Ce Hao, Jieji Ren, Qiaojun Yu, Cewu Lu, Yu Qiao, Jiangmiao Pang

Master Micro Residual Correction with Adaptive Tactile Fusion and Force-Mixed Control for Contact-Rich Manipulation

Abstract (English): Robotic contact-rich and fine-grained manipulation remains a significant challenge due to complex interaction dynamics and the competing requirements of multi-timescale control. While current visual imitation learning methods excel at long-horizon planning, they often fail to perceive critical interaction cues like friction variations or incipient slip, and struggle to balance global task coherence with local reactive feedback. To address these challenges, we propose M2-ResiPolicy, a novel Master-Micro residual control architecture that synergizes high-level action guidance with low-level correction. The framework consists of a Master-Guidance Policy (MGP) operating at 10 Hz, which generates temporally consistent action chunks via a diffusion-based backbone and employs a tactile-intensity-driven adaptive fusion mechanism to dynamically modulate perceptual weights between vision and touch. Simultaneously, a high-frequency (60 Hz) Micro-Residual Corrector (MRC) utilizes a lightweight GRU to provide real-time action compensation based on TCP wrench feedback. This policy is further integrated with a force-mixed PBIC execution layer, effectively regulating contact forces to ensure interaction safety. Experiments across several demanding tasks including fragile object grasping and precision insertion, demonstrate that M2-ResiPolicy significantly outperforms standard Diffusion Policy (DP) and state-of-the-art Reactive Diffusion Policy (RDP), achieving a 93\% damage-free success rate in chip grasping and superior force regulation stability.

摘要（中文）: 由于复杂的交互动力学和多时间尺度控制的竞争性需求，富接触型和精细化机器人操作仍然是一个重大挑战。虽然现有的视觉模仿学习方法擅长长程规划，但它们往往无法感知关键的交互线索，如摩擦变化或初始滑动，并且难以平衡全局任务连贯性与局部反应性反馈。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了一种新颖的主-微剩余控制架构M2-ResiPolicy，它协同了高层动作指导和低层校正。该框架由一个以10 Hz运行的主导向策略（MGP）组成，该策略通过基于扩散的主干网络生成时间一致的动作块，并采用触觉强度驱动的自适应融合机制来动态地调节视觉和触觉之间的感知权重。同时，一个高频率（60 Hz）的微剩余校正器（MRC）利用一个轻量级GRU，基于TCP扳手反馈提供实时的动作补偿。该策略进一步与力混合的PBIC执行层集成，有效地调节接触力以确保交互安全。在包括脆弱物体抓取和精密插入在内的几个高难度任务中的实验表明，M2-ResiPolicy显著优于标准扩散策略（DP）和最先进的反应式扩散策略（RDP），在芯片抓取中实现了93%的无损成功率，并具有卓越的力调节稳定性。

TLDR: The paper presents M2-ResiPolicy, a novel hierarchical control architecture for contact-rich robotic manipulation that combines diffusion-based high-level planning with low-level force feedback, demonstrating improved performance in fragile object grasping and precision insertion tasks.

简要说明: 该论文提出了一种名为M2-ResiPolicy的新型分层控制架构，用于接触丰富的机器人操作。它结合了基于扩散的高级规划与低级力反馈，展示了在易碎物体抓取和精密插入任务中的改进性能。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)

Authors: Xingting Li, Yifan Xie, Han Liu, Wei Hou, Guangyu Chen, Shoujie Li, Wenbo Ding

Confusion-Aware In-Context-Learning for Vision-Language Models in Robotic Manipulation

Abstract (English): Vision-language models (VLMs) have significantly improved the generalization capabilities of robotic manipulation. However, VLM-based systems often suffer from a lack of robustness, leading to unpredictable errors, particularly in scenarios involving confusable objects. Our preliminary analysis reveals that these failures are mainly caused by shortcut learning problem inherently in VLMs, limiting their ability to accurately distinguish between confusable features. To this end, we propose Confusion-Aware In-Context Learning (CAICL), a method that enhances VLM performance in confusable scenarios for robotic manipulation. The approach begins with confusion localization and analysis, identifying potential sources of confusion. This information is then used as a prompt for the VLM to focus on features most likely to cause misidentification. Extensive experiments on the VIMA-Bench show that CAICL effectively addresses the shortcut learning issue, achieving a 85.5\% success rate and showing good stability across tasks with different degrees of generalization.

摘要（中文）: 视觉-语言模型（VLMs）已显著提升了机器人操作的泛化能力。然而，基于VLM的系统常常缺乏鲁棒性，导致不可预测的错误，尤其是在涉及易混淆对象的场景中。我们的初步分析表明，这些失败主要是由VLM中固有的捷径学习问题引起的，这限制了它们准确区分易混淆特征的能力。为此，我们提出了一种混淆感知上下文学习（CAICL）方法，以增强VLM在机器人操作中易混淆场景下的性能。该方法首先进行混淆定位和分析，识别潜在的混淆来源。然后，将此信息用作VLM的提示，使其专注于最容易导致误认的特征。在VIMA-Bench上的大量实验表明，CAICL有效地解决了捷径学习问题，实现了85.5%的成功率，并在具有不同泛化程度的任务中表现出良好的稳定性。

TLDR: This paper introduces Confusion-Aware In-Context Learning (CAICL) to improve the performance of Vision-Language Models in robotic manipulation tasks involving confusable objects, achieving a significant success rate improvement on the VIMA-Bench.

简要说明: 本文提出了一种名为Confusion-Aware In-Context Learning (CAICL) 的方法，旨在提高视觉-语言模型在机器人操作任务中处理易混淆对象时的性能，并在VIMA-Bench测试中取得了显著的成功率提升。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Authors: Yayun He, Zuheng Kang, Botao Zhao, Zhouyin Wu, Junqing Peng, Jianzong Wang

HALO:Closing Sim-to-Real Gap for Heavy-loaded Humanoid Agile Motion Skills via Differentiable Simulation

Abstract (English): Humanoid robots deployed in real-world scenarios often need to carry unknown payloads, which introduce significant mismatch and degrade the effectiveness of simulation-to-reality reinforcement learning methods. To address this challenge, we propose a two-stage gradient-based system identification framework built on the differentiable simulator MuJoCo XLA. The first stage calibrates the nominal robot model using real-world data to reduce intrinsic sim-to-real discrepancies, while the second stage further identifies the mass distribution of the unknown payload. By explicitly reducing structured model bias prior to policy training, our approach enables zero-shot transfer of reinforcement learning policies to hardware under heavy-load conditions. Extensive simulation and real-world experiments demonstrate more precise parameter identification, improved motion tracking accuracy, and substantially enhanced agility and robustness compared to existing baselines. Project Page: https://mwondering.github.io/halo-humanoid/

摘要（中文）: 真实世界场景中部署的类人机器人经常需要携带未知的有效载荷，这会引入显著的不匹配并降低从仿真到现实强化学习方法的有效性。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种基于可微分仿真器MuJoCo XLA构建的两阶段梯度系统辨识框架。第一阶段使用真实世界数据校准标称机器人模型，以减少固有的仿真-现实差异，而第二阶段进一步辨识未知有效载荷的质量分布。通过在策略训练之前显式地减少结构化模型偏差，我们的方法能够在重载条件下实现强化学习策略到硬件的零样本迁移。大量的仿真和真实世界实验表明，与现有基线相比，我们的方法具有更精确的参数辨识、改进的运动跟踪精度以及显著增强的敏捷性和鲁棒性。项目主页：https://mwondering.github.io/halo-humanoid/

TLDR: This paper presents a two-stage gradient-based system identification framework using a differentiable simulator to calibrate humanoid robot models and identify unknown payloads, enabling zero-shot sim-to-real transfer of RL policies under heavy-load conditions.

简要说明: 本文提出了一种两阶段的基于梯度的系统辨识框架，使用可微模拟器来校准人形机器人模型并识别未知的有效载荷，从而在重载条件下实现强化学习策略的零样本模拟到真实世界的迁移。

Authors: Xingyi Wang, Chenyun Zhang, Weiji Xie, Chao Yu, Wei Song, Chenjia Bai, Shiqiang Zhu

CycleRL: Sim-to-Real Deep Reinforcement Learning for Robust Autonomous Bicycle Control

Abstract (English): Autonomous bicycles offer a promising agile solution for urban mobility and last-mile logistics, however, conventional control strategies often struggle with their underactuated nonlinear dynamics, suffering from sensitivity to model mismatches and limited adaptability to real-world uncertainties. To address this, this paper presents CycleRL, the first sim-to-real deep reinforcement learning framework designed for robust autonomous bicycle control. Our approach trains an end-to-end neural control policy within the high-fidelity NVIDIA Isaac Sim environment, leveraging Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to circumvent the need for an explicit dynamics model. The framework features a composite reward function tailored for concurrent balance maintenance, velocity tracking, and steering control. Crucially, systematic domain randomization is employed to bridge the simulation-to-reality gap and facilitate direct transfer. In simulation, CycleRL achieves considerable performance, including a 99.90% balance success rate, a low steering tracking error of 1.15°, and a velocity tracking error of 0.18 m/s. These quantitative results, coupled with successful hardware transfer, validate DRL as an effective paradigm for autonomous bicycle control, offering superior adaptability over traditional methods. Video demonstrations are available at https://anony6f05.github.io/CycleRL/.

摘要（中文）: 自动驾驶自行车为城市交通和最后一公里物流提供了一种有希望的敏捷解决方案，然而，传统的控制策略通常难以应对其欠驱动的非线性动力学特性，容易受到模型失配的影响，并且对现实世界的不确定性的适应性有限。为了解决这个问题，本文提出了CycleRL，这是一个为鲁棒的自动驾驶自行车控制而设计的首个从仿真到实体的深度强化学习框架。我们的方法在高保真NVIDIA Isaac Sim环境下训练一个端到端的神经控制策略，利用近端策略优化（PPO）来避免对显式动力学模型的需求。该框架具有一个为并发的平衡维持、速度跟踪和转向控制而定制的复合奖励函数。至关重要的是，采用系统的域随机化来弥合仿真到现实的差距并促进直接迁移。在仿真中，CycleRL取得了显著的性能，包括99.90%的平衡成功率，1.15°的低转向跟踪误差以及0.18 m/s的速度跟踪误差。这些定量结果，加上成功的硬件迁移，验证了DRL作为自动驾驶自行车控制的有效范例，与传统方法相比，具有卓越的适应性。视频演示可在https://anony6f05.github.io/CycleRL/上找到。

TLDR: The paper introduces CycleRL, a sim-to-real deep reinforcement learning framework for autonomous bicycle control, demonstrating successful transfer to hardware using PPO and domain randomization.

简要说明: 本文介绍了CycleRL，一个用于自动自行车控制的sim-to-real深度强化学习框架，通过PPO和领域随机化实现了向硬件的成功迁移。

Authors: Gelu Liu, Teng Wang, Zhijie Wu, Junliang Wu, Songyuan Li, Xiangwei Zhu

ReMAP-DP: Reprojected Multi-view Aligned PointMaps for Diffusion Policy

Abstract (English): Generalist robot policies built upon 2D visual representations excel at semantic reasoning but inherently lack the explicit 3D spatial awareness required for high-precision tasks. Existing 3D integration methods struggle to bridge this gap due to the structural irregularity of sparse point clouds and the geometric distortion introduced by multi-view orthographic rendering. To overcome these barriers, we present ReMAP-DP, a novel framework synergizing standardized perspective reprojection with a structure-aware dual-stream diffusion policy. By coupling the re-projected views with pixel-aligned PointMaps, our dual-stream architecture leverages learnable modality embeddings to fuse frozen semantic features and explicit geometric descriptors, ensuring precise implicit patch-level alignment. Extensive experiments across simulation and real-world environments demonstrate ReMAP-DP's superior performance in diverse manipulation tasks. On RoboTwin 2.0, it attains a 59.3% average success rate, outperforming the DP3 baseline by +6.6%. On ManiSkill 3, our method yields a 28% improvement over DP3 on the geometrically challenging Stack Cube task. Furthermore, ReMAP-DP exhibits remarkable real-world robustness, executing high-precision and dynamic manipulations with superior data efficiency from only a handful of demonstrations. Project page is available at: https://icr-lab.github.io/ReMAP-DP/

摘要（中文）: 基于2D视觉表征的通用机器人策略在语义推理方面表现出色，但天生缺乏高精度任务所需的显式3D空间感知能力。由于稀疏点云的结构不规则性和多视角正交投影引入的几何畸变，现有的3D集成方法难以弥合这一差距。为了克服这些障碍，我们提出ReMAP-DP，一种将标准化透视重投影与结构感知双流扩散策略相结合的新颖框架。通过将重投影视图与像素对齐的PointMap相结合，我们的双流架构利用可学习的模态嵌入来融合冻结的语义特征和显式几何描述符，确保精确的隐式patch级对齐。在模拟和真实环境中的大量实验表明，ReMAP-DP在各种操作任务中表现出卓越的性能。在RoboTwin 2.0上，它获得了59.3%的平均成功率，比DP3基线提高了+6.6%。在ManiSkill 3上，我们的方法在几何挑战性的堆叠立方体任务中比DP3提高了28%。此外，ReMAP-DP表现出卓越的真实世界鲁棒性，仅通过少量示例即可高效执行高精度和动态操作。项目页面请访问：https://icr-lab.github.io/ReMAP-DP/

TLDR: ReMAP-DP is a novel framework using reprojected multi-view aligned pointmaps with a dual-stream diffusion policy to improve robot manipulation, achieving superior performance in simulation and real-world environments compared to baseline methods.

简要说明: ReMAP-DP 是一种新颖的框架，使用重投影的多视图对齐点图与双流扩散策略来改进机器人操作，与基线方法相比，在模拟和现实环境中实现了卓越的性能。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Xinzhang Yang, Renjun Wu, Jinyan Liu, Xuesong Li

Learning from Mistakes: Post-Training for Driving VLA with Takeover Data

Abstract (English): Current Vision-Language-Action (VLA) paradigms in end-to-end autonomous driving rely on offline training from static datasets, leaving them vulnerable to distribution shift. Recent post-training methods use takeover data to mitigate this by augmenting the dataset with high-quality expert takeover samples, yet they suffer from two key limitations: supervision restricted to the period after the takeover moments leads to policies with limited safety margins, and passive preference optimization lacks active exploration for optimal performance. In this paper, we propose TakeVLA, a novel VLA post-training framework that overcomes these shortcomings through two complementary innovations. First, we introduce pre-takeover language supervision, which allows the VLA to learn from mistakes proactively. By explicitly teaching the model about what to do in error-prone situations, we cultivate a precautionary mindset that anticipates hazards early and substantially enlarges safety margins. Second, we propose Scenario Dreaming, a reinforcement fine-tuning paradigm that operates in reconstruceted takeover scenarios, encouraging active exploration beyond mere preference fitting. Experiments on the Bench2Drive benchmark demonstrate that TakeVLA achieves state-of-the-art closed-loop performance, surpassing the strong VLA baseline SimLingo by 4.93 in driving score, with an enhanced safety margin as evidenced by an 11.76% increase in average TTC.

摘要（中文）: 端到端自动驾驶中当前的视觉-语言-动作（VLA）范式依赖于静态数据集上的离线训练，使其容易受到分布偏移的影响。最近的后训练方法通过使用高质量的专家接管样本来扩充数据集，从而利用接管数据来缓解这个问题，但它们受到两个关键限制：对仅限于接管时刻之后的时段的监督导致策略的安全裕度有限，以及被动的偏好优化缺乏对最佳性能的主动探索。在本文中，我们提出了TakeVLA，一种新颖的VLA后训练框架，通过两个互补的创新来克服这些缺点。首先，我们引入了接管前语言监督，这使得VLA能够主动地从错误中学习。通过显式地教导模型在易出错的情况下该怎么做，我们培养了一种预防性思维，可以及早地预测风险，并大大增加安全裕度。其次，我们提出了情景梦想（Scenario Dreaming），一种在重构的接管情景中运行的强化微调范式，鼓励超越单纯偏好拟合的主动探索。在Bench2Drive基准上的实验表明，TakeVLA实现了最先进的闭环性能，在驾驶评分方面超越了强大的VLA基线SimLingo 4.93分，并且安全裕度得到了增强，平均碰撞时间（TTC）增加了11.76%证明了这一点。

TLDR: The paper introduces TakeVLA, a post-training framework for autonomous driving VLA models that uses pre-takeover language supervision and scenario dreaming to improve safety and performance, demonstrating state-of-the-art results on the Bench2Drive benchmark.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了TakeVLA，一个用于自动驾驶VLA模型的后训练框架，它使用接管前语言监督和场景梦想来提高安全性和性能，并在Bench2Drive基准测试中展示了最先进的结果。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yinfeng Gao, Deqing Liu, Qichao Zhang, Yupeng Zheng, Haochen Tian, Guang Li, Hangjun Ye, Long Chen, Da-Wei Ding, Dongbin Zhao

PerlAD: Towards Enhanced Closed-loop End-to-end Autonomous Driving with Pseudo-simulation-based Reinforcement Learning

Abstract (English): End-to-end autonomous driving policies based on Imitation Learning (IL) often struggle in closed-loop execution due to the misalignment between inadequate open-loop training objectives and real driving requirements. While Reinforcement Learning (RL) offers a solution by directly optimizing driving goals via reward signals, the rendering-based training environments introduce the rendering gap and are inefficient due to high computational costs. To overcome these challenges, we present a novel Pseudo-simulation-based RL method for closed-loop end-to-end autonomous driving, PerlAD. Based on offline datasets, PerlAD constructs a pseudo-simulation that operates in vector space, enabling efficient, rendering-free trial-and-error training. To bridge the gap between static datasets and dynamic closed-loop environments, PerlAD introduces a prediction world model that generates reactive agent trajectories conditioned on the ego vehicle's plan. Furthermore, to facilitate efficient planning, PerlAD utilizes a hierarchical decoupled planner that combines IL for lateral path generation and RL for longitudinal speed optimization. Comprehensive experimental results demonstrate that PerlAD achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Bench2Drive benchmark, surpassing the previous E2E RL method by 10.29% in Driving Score without requiring expensive online interactions. Additional evaluations on the DOS benchmark further confirm its reliability in handling safety-critical occlusion scenarios.

摘要（中文）: 基于模仿学习(IL)的端到端自动驾驶策略在闭环执行中常常表现不佳，这是由于不充分的开环训练目标与真实的驾驶需求之间存在偏差。强化学习(RL)通过奖励信号直接优化驾驶目标，提供了一种解决方案，但基于渲染的训练环境引入了渲染鸿沟，并且由于高昂的计算成本效率低下。为了克服这些挑战，我们提出了一种新颖的基于伪仿真的闭环端到端自动驾驶强化学习方法，PerlAD。PerlAD基于离线数据集构建了一个在向量空间中运行的伪仿真，从而能够进行高效的、无渲染的试错训练。为了弥合静态数据集和动态闭环环境之间的差距，PerlAD引入了一个预测世界模型，该模型根据自车计划生成反应式智能体轨迹。此外，为了方便高效规划，PerlAD采用了一种分层解耦规划器，它结合了用于横向路径生成的IL和用于纵向速度优化的RL。全面的实验结果表明，PerlAD在Bench2Drive基准测试上实现了最先进的性能，在驾驶得分方面超过了之前的E2E RL方法10.29%，且无需昂贵的在线交互。在DOS基准上的额外评估进一步证实了其在处理安全关键的遮挡场景中的可靠性。

TLDR: PerlAD is a new end-to-end autonomous driving method that uses pseudo-simulation and reinforcement learning to achieve state-of-the-art performance on driving benchmarks, addressing the limitations of imitation learning and rendering-based RL.

简要说明: PerlAD 是一种新的端到端自动驾驶方法，它使用伪模拟和强化学习，在驾驶基准测试中实现了最先进的性能，解决了模仿学习和基于渲染的强化学习的局限性。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)

Authors: Yinfeng Gao, Qichao Zhang, Deqing Liu, Zhongpu Xia, Guang Li, Kun Ma, Guang Chen, Hangjun Ye, Long Chen, Da-Wei Ding, Dongbin Zhao

Ego to World: Collaborative Spatial Reasoning in Embodied Systems via Reinforcement Learning

Abstract (English): Understanding the world from distributed, partial viewpoints is a fundamental challenge for embodied multi-agent systems. Each agent perceives the environment through an ego-centric view that is often limited by occlusion and ambiguity. To study this problem, we introduce the Ego-to-World (E2W) benchmark, which evaluates a vision-language model's ability to fuse heterogeneous viewpoints across three tasks: (i) global counting, (ii) relational location reasoning, and (iii) action-oriented grasping that requires predicting view-specific image coordinates. To address this setting, we propose CoRL, a two-stage framework that combines Chain-of-Thought supervised fine-tuning with reinforcement learning using Group-Relative Policy Optimization. Its core component, the Cross-View Spatial Reward (CVSR), provides dense task-aligned feedback by linking reasoning steps to visual evidence, ensuring coherent cross-view entity resolution, and guiding the model toward correct final predictions. Experiments on E2W show that CoRL consistently surpasses strong proprietary and open-source baselines on both reasoning and perception-grounding metrics, while ablations further confirm the necessity of each CVSR component. Beyond that, CoRL generalizes to external spatial reasoning benchmarks and enables effective real-world multi-robot manipulation with calibrated multi-camera rigs, demonstrating cross-view localization and successful grasp-and-place execution. Together, E2W and CoRL provide a principled foundation for learning world-centric scene understanding from distributed, ego-centric observations, advancing collaborative embodied AI.

摘要（中文）: 从分布式、局部视角理解世界是具身多智能体系统面临的一个根本性挑战。每个智能体通过自我中心的视角感知环境，而这种视角常常受到遮挡和歧义的限制。为了研究这个问题，我们引入了Ego-to-World (E2W) 基准，该基准评估视觉-语言模型融合不同视角的能力，通过三个任务：(i) 全局计数；(ii) 关系位置推理；以及 (iii) 面向动作的抓取，该抓取需要预测特定视角的图像坐标。为了应对这种设置，我们提出了CoRL，这是一个两阶段框架，结合了思维链的监督微调和使用组相对策略优化的强化学习。其核心组件，跨视角空间奖励（CVSR），通过将推理步骤与视觉证据联系起来，提供了密集的任务对齐型反馈，确保连贯的跨视角实体解析，并引导模型做出正确的最终预测。在E2W上的实验表明，CoRL在推理和感知-定位指标上始终超越了强大的专有和开源基线，而消融实验进一步证实了每个CVSR组件的必要性。除此之外，CoRL泛化到外部空间推理基准，并能够在校准的多摄像头装置下实现有效的真实世界多机器人操作，展示了跨视角定位和成功的抓取-放置执行。总而言之，E2W和CoRL为从分布式、自我中心的观察中学习以世界为中心的场景理解提供了原则性的基础，从而推进了协作式具身人工智能。

TLDR: The paper introduces the Ego-to-World (E2W) benchmark for embodied multi-agent systems, addressing scene understanding from distributed viewpoints, and proposes a two-stage framework, CoRL, combining Chain-of-Thought with reinforcement learning, achieving state-of-the-art results on the benchmark and generalizing to real-world robotic manipulation.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了Ego-to-World (E2W) 基准测试，用于研究具身多智能体系统从分布式视角进行场景理解的问题，并提出了一个两阶段框架CoRL，该框架结合了思维链和强化学习，在基准测试中取得了最先进的结果，并推广到真实世界的机器人操作。

Authors: Heng Zhou, Li Kang, Yiran Qin, Xiufeng Song, Ao Yu, Zilu Zhang, Haoming Song, Kaixin Xu, Yuchen Fan, Dongzhan Zhou, Xiaohong Liu, Ruimao Zhang, Philip Torr, Lei Bai, Zhenfei Yin

HiMemVLN: Enhancing Reliability of Open-Source Zero-Shot Vision-and-Language Navigation with Hierarchical Memory System

Abstract (English): LLM-based agents have demonstrated impressive zero-shot performance in vision-language navigation (VLN) tasks. However, most zero-shot methods primarily rely on closed-source LLMs as navigators, which face challenges related to high token costs and potential data leakage risks. Recent efforts have attempted to address this by using open-source LLMs combined with a spatiotemporal CoT framework, but they still fall far short compared to closed-source models. In this work, we identify a critical issue, Navigation Amnesia, through a detailed analysis of the navigation process. This issue leads to navigation failures and amplifies the gap between open-source and closed-source methods. To address this, we propose HiMemVLN, which incorporates a Hierarchical Memory System into a multimodal large model to enhance visual perception recall and long-term localization, mitigating the amnesia issue and improving the agent's navigation performance. Extensive experiments in both simulated and real-world environments demonstrate that HiMemVLN achieves nearly twice the performance of the open-source state-of-the-art method. The code is available at https://github.com/lvkailin0118/HiMemVLN.

摘要（中文）: 基于LLM的智能体在视觉语言导航(VLN)任务中展现出了令人印象深刻的零样本性能。然而，大多数零样本方法主要依赖于闭源LLM作为导航器，这面临着高昂的token成本和潜在的数据泄露风险等挑战。最近的研究尝试通过使用开源LLM结合时空CoT框架来解决这个问题，但与闭源模型相比，它们仍然相差甚远。在这项工作中，我们通过对导航过程的详细分析，发现了一个关键问题，即导航失忆症。这个问题会导致导航失败，并扩大开源方法和闭源方法之间的差距。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了HiMemVLN，它将一个分层记忆系统集成到多模态大型模型中，以增强视觉感知回忆和长期定位能力，从而减轻失忆症问题，提高智能体的导航性能。在模拟和真实世界环境中进行的广泛实验表明，HiMemVLN的性能几乎是当前最先进的开源方法的两倍。代码可在 https://github.com/lvkailin0118/HiMemVLN 获取。

TLDR: The paper introduces HiMemVLN, a hierarchical memory system to enhance the reliability of open-source vision-language navigation agents by addressing the 'Navigation Amnesia' issue, significantly improving performance over existing open-source methods.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了HiMemVLN，一种分层记忆系统，旨在通过解决“导航失忆症”问题来提高开源视觉语言导航代理的可靠性，与现有的开源方法相比，性能显著提高。
