Authors: Andreas Rausch, Stefan Wittek

A Self-Evolving Defect Detection Framework for Industrial Photovoltaic Systems

Abstract (English): Reliable photovoltaic (PV) power generation requires timely detection of module defects that may reduce energy yield, accelerate degradation, and increase lifecycle operation and maintenance costs during field operation. Electroluminescence (EL) imaging has therefore been widely adopted for PV module inspection. However, automated defect detection in real operational environments remains challenging due to heterogeneous module geometries, low-resolution imaging conditions, subtle defect morphology, long-tailed defect distributions, and continual data shifts introduced by evolving inspection and labeling processes. These factors significantly limit the robustness and long-term maintainability of conventional deep-learning inspection pipelines. To address these challenges, this paper proposes SEPDD, a Self-Evolving Photovoltaic Defect Detection framework designed for evolving industrial PV inspection scenarios. SEPDD integrates automated model optimization with a continual self-evolving learning mechanism, enabling the inspection system to progressively adapt to distribution shifts and newly emerging defect patterns during long-term deployment. Experiments conducted on both a public PV defect benchmark and a private industrial EL dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework. Both datasets exhibit severe class imbalance and significant domain shift. SEPDD achieves a leading mAP50 of 91.4% on the public dataset and 49.5% on the private dataset. It surpasses the autonomous baseline by 14.8% and human experts by 4.7% on the public dataset, and by 4.9% and 2.5%, respectively, on the private dataset.

摘要（中文）: 摘要： 可靠的光伏发电需要及时检测组件缺陷，这些缺陷可能降低能量产出、加速衰退，并增加现场运行期间生命周期运营和维护成本。因此，电致发光 (EL) 成像已被广泛应用于光伏组件检测。然而，由于组件几何形状各异、低分辨率成像条件、细微的缺陷形态、长尾缺陷分布以及不断变化的检测和标注流程引入的持续数据漂移，在实际运行环境中进行自动化缺陷检测仍然具有挑战性。这些因素显著限制了传统深度学习检测流程的鲁棒性和长期可维护性。为了应对这些挑战，本文提出了一种名为SEPDD的自进化光伏缺陷检测框架，专为不断发展的工业光伏检测场景而设计。SEPDD集成了自动化模型优化与持续自进化学习机制，使检测系统能够在长期部署过程中逐步适应分布漂移和新出现的缺陷模式。在公共光伏缺陷基准和私有的工业EL数据集上进行的实验证明了所提出的框架的有效性。这两个数据集都表现出严重的类别不平衡和显著的领域漂移。SEPDD在公共数据集上实现了91.4%的领先mAP50，在私有数据集上实现了49.5%。在公共数据集上，它比自主基线高出14.8%，比人类专家高出4.7%；而在私有数据集上，分别高出4.9%和2.5%。

TLDR: The paper proposes a self-evolving defect detection framework (SEPDD) for photovoltaic systems using electroluminescence imaging, addressing challenges related to data shifts and long-tailed defect distributions by integrating automated model optimization with continual learning. It demonstrates superior performance compared to baselines and human experts on public and private datasets.

简要说明: 该论文提出了一种用于光伏系统的自进化缺陷检测框架(SEPDD)，使用电致发光成像，通过将自动模型优化与持续学习相结合，解决了与数据偏移和长尾缺陷分布相关的挑战。在公开和私有数据集上，相对于基线和人类专家，该框架表现出卓越的性能。

Relevance: (2/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Authors: Haoyu He, Yu Duan, Wenzhen Liu, Hanyuan Hang, Qiantu Tuo, Xiaoke Yang, Rui Li

Context-Aware Sensor Modeling for Asynchronous Multi-Sensor Tracking in Stone Soup

Abstract (English): Multi-sensor tracking in the real world involves asynchronous sensors with partial coverage and heterogeneous detection performance. Although probabilistic tracking methods permit detection probability and clutter intensity to depend on state and sensing context, many practical frameworks enforce globally uniform observability assumptions. Under multi-rate and partially overlapping sensing, this simplification causes repeated non-detections from high-rate sensors to erode tracks visible only to low-rate sensors, potentially degrading fusion performance. We introduce DetectorContext, an abstraction for the open-source multi-target tracking framework Stone Soup. DetectorContext exposes detection probability and clutter intensity as state-dependent functions evaluated during hypothesis formation. The abstraction integrates with existing probabilistic trackers without modifying their update equations. Experiments on asynchronous radar-lidar data demonstrate that context-aware modeling restores stable fusion and significantly improves HOTA and GOSPA performance without increasing false tracks.

摘要（中文）: 现实世界中的多传感器跟踪涉及异步传感器，这些传感器具有局部覆盖和异构的检测性能。虽然概率跟踪方法允许检测概率和杂波强度依赖于状态和感知上下文，但许多实际框架强制执行全局统一的可观测性假设。在多速率和部分重叠感知的情况下，这种简化会导致来自高速率传感器的重复未检测侵蚀仅对低速率传感器可见的航迹，从而可能降低融合性能。 我们引入了DetectorContext，一种用于开源多目标跟踪框架Stone Soup的抽象概念。DetectorContext将检测概率和杂波强度作为状态相关函数暴露出来，并在假设形成过程中进行评估。该抽象概念与现有的概率跟踪器集成，而无需修改它们的更新方程。对异步雷达-激光雷达数据的实验表明，上下文感知的建模恢复了稳定的融合，并在不增加虚假航迹的情况下显著提高了HOTA和GOSPA性能。

TLDR: The paper introduces DetectorContext, a Stone Soup abstraction, to address track erosion in asynchronous multi-sensor tracking by modeling state-dependent detection probability and clutter, leading to improved tracking performance in radar-lidar fusion.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种Stone Soup抽象类 DetectorContext，通过建模状态相关的检测概率和杂波，解决异步多传感器跟踪中的轨迹侵蚀问题，从而提高了雷达-激光雷达融合的跟踪性能。

Relevance: (3/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (8/10)

Authors: Martin Vonheim Larsen, Kim Mathiassen

$\text{F}^2\text{HDR}$: Two-Stage HDR Video Reconstruction via Flow Adapter and Physical Motion Modeling

Abstract (English): Reconstructing High Dynamic Range (HDR) videos from sequences of alternating-exposure Low Dynamic Range (LDR) frames remains highly challenging, especially under dynamic scenes where cross-exposure inconsistencies and complex motion make inter-frame alignment difficult, leading to ghosting and detail loss. Existing methods often suffer from inaccurate alignment, suboptimal feature aggregation, and degraded reconstruction quality in motion-dominated regions. To address these challenges, we propose $\text{F}^2\text{HDR}$, a two-stage HDR video reconstruction framework that robustly perceives inter-frame motion and restores fine details in complex dynamic scenarios. The proposed framework integrates a flow adapter that adapts generic optical flow for robust cross-exposure alignment, a physical motion modeling to identify salient motion regions, and a motion-aware refinement network that aggregates complementary information while removing ghosting and noise. Extensive experiments demonstrate that $\text{F}^2\text{HDR}$ achieves state-of-the-art performance on real-world HDR video benchmarks, producing ghost-free and high-fidelity results under large motion and exposure variations.

摘要（中文）: 从交替曝光的低动态范围（LDR）帧序列中重建高动态范围（HDR）视频仍然极具挑战性，尤其是在动态场景下，跨曝光不一致性和复杂运动使得帧间对齐变得困难，从而导致重影和细节丢失。现有方法通常存在不准确的对齐、次优的特征聚合以及在运动主导区域中降低的重建质量等问题。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出$\text{F}^2\text{HDR}$，一种两阶段HDR视频重建框架，能够稳健地感知帧间运动并在复杂的动态场景中恢复精细细节。该框架集成了一个流动适配器，用于调整通用光流以实现稳健的跨曝光对齐；一个物理运动建模，用于识别显著运动区域；以及一个运动感知精细化网络，其聚合互补信息，同时消除重影和噪声。大量实验表明，$\text{F}^2\text{HDR}$在真实HDR视频基准上实现了最先进的性能，在大的运动和曝光变化下产生无重影和高保真的结果。

TLDR: The paper introduces F^2HDR, a two-stage framework for reconstructing HDR videos from LDR sequences, using flow adaptation and physical motion modeling to address challenges in dynamic scenes with significant motion and exposure variations.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了F^2HDR，一个用于从LDR序列重建HDR视频的两阶段框架，利用光流自适应和物理运动建模来解决动态场景中运动和曝光变化带来的挑战。

Relevance: (2/10)

Authors: Huanjing Yue, Dawei Li, Shaoxiong Tu, Jingyu Yang

Active Seriation: Efficient Ordering Recovery with Statistical Guarantees

Abstract (English): Active seriation aims at recovering an unknown ordering of $n$ items by adaptively querying pairwise similarities. The observations are noisy measurements of entries of an underlying $n$ x $n$ permuted Robinson matrix, whose permutation encodes the latent ordering. The framework allows the algorithm to start with partial information on the latent ordering, including seriation from scratch as a special case. We propose an active seriation algorithm that provably recovers the latent ordering with high probability. Under a uniform separation condition on the similarity matrix, optimal performance guarantees are established, both in terms of the probability of error and the number of observations required for successful recovery.

摘要（中文）: 主动排序旨在通过自适应地查询成对相似度来恢复 $n$ 个项目的未知排序。观测结果是对一个潜在 $n$ x $n$ 置换 Robinson 矩阵条目的噪声测量，该矩阵的置换编码了潜在的排序。该框架允许算法从潜在排序的部分信息开始，包括从头开始排序作为一种特殊情况。我们提出了一种主动排序算法，可以证明它以高概率恢复潜在排序。在相似度矩阵的均匀分离条件下，建立了最优的性能保证，包括误差概率和成功恢复所需的观测次数。

TLDR: This paper introduces an active learning algorithm for recovering an unknown ordering of items by adaptively querying pairwise similarities, achieving provable recovery guarantees under certain conditions.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种主动学习算法，通过自适应地查询成对相似性来恢复物品的未知排序，并在特定条件下获得了可证明的恢复保证。

Authors: James Cheshire, Yann Issartel

PiGRAND: Physics-informed Graph Neural Diffusion for Intelligent Additive Manufacturing

Abstract (English): A comprehensive understanding of heat transport is essential for optimizing various mechanical and engineering applications, including 3D printing. Recent advances in machine learning, combined with physics-based models, have enabled a powerful fusion of numerical methods and data-driven algorithms. This progress is driven by the availability of limited sensor data in various engineering and scientific domains, where the cost of data collection and the inaccessibility of certain measurements are high. To this end, we present PiGRAND, a Physics-informed graph neural diffusion framework. In order to reduce the computational complexity of graph learning, an efficient graph construction procedure was developed. Our approach is inspired by the explicit Euler and implicit Crank-Nicolson methods for modeling continuous heat transport, leveraging sub-learning models to secure the accurate diffusion across graph nodes. To enhance computational performance, our approach is combined with efficient transfer learning. We evaluate PiGRAND on thermal images from 3D printing, demonstrating significant improvements in prediction accuracy and computational performance compared to traditional graph neural diffusion (GRAND) and physics-informed neural networks (PINNs). These enhancements are attributed to the incorporation of physical principles derived from the theoretical study of partial differential equations (PDEs) into the learning model. The PiGRAND code is open-sourced on GitHub: https://github.com/bu32loxa/PiGRAND

摘要（中文）: 热传输的全面理解对于优化各种机械和工程应用至关重要，包括3D打印。机器学习的最新进展与基于物理的模型相结合，实现了数值方法和数据驱动算法的强大融合。这种进步得益于各种工程和科学领域中有限的传感器数据的可用性，其中数据收集成本高昂且某些测量难以进行。为此，我们提出了一种物理信息图神经网络扩散框架PiGRAND。为了降低图学习的计算复杂度，我们开发了一种高效的图构建过程。我们的方法受到显式欧拉法和隐式 Crank-Nicolson 方法的启发，用于建模连续热传输，利用子学习模型来确保图节点间的精确扩散。为了提高计算性能，我们的方法与高效的迁移学习相结合。我们在3D打印的热图像上评估了PiGRAND，与传统的图神经网络扩散（GRAND）和物理信息神经网络（PINN）相比，在预测精度和计算性能方面都显示出显著的提升。这些提升归功于将源自偏微分方程（PDE）理论研究的物理原理纳入学习模型。PiGRAND代码已在GitHub上开源：https://github.com/bu32loxa/PiGRAND

TLDR: PiGRAND introduces a Physics-informed Graph Neural Diffusion framework for improved heat transport modeling in 3D printing, achieving better accuracy and computational performance than existing methods by integrating physical principles into graph neural networks.

简要说明: PiGRAND 提出了一种物理信息图神经网络扩散框架，用于改进 3D 打印中的热传输建模，通过将物理原理集成到图神经网络中，实现了比现有方法更高的精度和计算性能。

Authors: Benjamin Uhrich, Tim Häntschel, Erhard Rahm

The impact of machine learning forecasting on strategic decision-making for Bike Sharing Systems

Abstract (English): In this paper, machine learning techniques are used to forecast the difference between bike returns and withdrawals at each station of a bike sharing system. The forecasts are integrated into a simulation framework that is used to support long-term decisions and model the daily dynamics, including the relocation of bikes. We assess the quality of the machine learning-based forecasts in two ways. Firstly, we compare the forecasts with alternative prediction methods. Secondly, we analyze the impact of the forecasts on the quality of the output of the simulation framework. The evaluation is based on real-world data of the bike sharing system currently operating in Brescia, Italy.

摘要（中文）: 本文利用机器学习技术预测自行车共享系统中每个站点的自行车归还量与借出量之差。这些预测结果被整合到一个仿真框架中，用于支持长期决策并建模每日动态，包括自行车的重新配置。我们通过两种方式评估基于机器学习的预测质量。首先，我们将预测结果与替代预测方法进行比较。其次，我们分析预测结果对仿真框架输出质量的影响。评估基于意大利布雷西亚当前运营的自行车共享系统的真实数据。

TLDR: This paper uses machine learning to forecast bike sharing system demand and integrates these forecasts into a simulation to support strategic decision-making, using real-world data from Brescia, Italy.

简要说明: 本文利用机器学习预测共享单车系统的需求，并将这些预测结果整合到仿真模型中，以支持战略决策，数据来自意大利布雷西亚的实际运营系统。

Authors: Enrico Angelelli, Andrea Mor, Carlotta Orsenigo, M. Grazia Speranza, Carlo Vercellis

On the Derivation of Tightly-Coupled LiDAR-Inertial Odometry with VoxelMap

Abstract (English): This note presents a concise mathematical formulation of tightly-coupled LiDAR-Inertial Odometry within an iterated error-state Kalman filter framework using a VoxelMap representation. Rather than proposing a new algorithm, it provides a clear and self-contained derivation that unifies the geometric modeling and probabilistic state estimation through consistent notation and explicit formulations. The document is intended to serve both as a technical reference and as an accessible entry point for a foundational understanding of the system architecture and estimation principles.

摘要（中文）: 本文档简洁地阐述了一种紧耦合的激光雷达惯性里程计的数学公式，该公式基于体素地图表示，并在迭代误差状态卡尔曼滤波器的框架下实现。本文档并非提出一种新的算法，而是提供了一个清晰且独立的推导过程，通过一致的符号和显式的公式，统一了几何建模和概率状态估计。本文档旨在作为技术参考和便于理解系统架构及估计原理的入门资料。

TLDR: The paper presents a concise mathematical formulation of tightly-coupled LiDAR-Inertial Odometry using a VoxelMap within an error-state Kalman filter framework, aiming to provide a clear and accessible derivation for foundational understanding.

简要说明: 该论文提出了一个基于VoxelMap的紧耦合LiDAR-惯性里程计的简洁数学公式，采用迭代误差状态卡尔曼滤波器框架，旨在为系统架构和估计原理提供清晰易懂的推导。

Relevance: (2/10)
Novelty: (3/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (5/10)
Overall: (3/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Zhihao Zhan

Vib2ECG: A Paired Chest-Lead SCG-ECG Dataset and Benchmark for ECG Reconstruction

Abstract (English): Twelve-lead electrocardiography (ECG) is essential for cardiovascular diagnosis, but its long-term acquisition in daily life is constrained by complex and costly hardware. Recent efforts have explored reconstructing ECG from low-cost cardiac vibrational signals such as seismocardiography (SCG), however, due to the lack of a dataset, current methods are limited to limb leads, while clinical diagnosis requires multi-lead ECG, including chest leads. In this work, we propose Vib2ECG, the first paired, multi-channel electro-mechanical cardiac signal dataset, which includes complete twelve-lead ECGs and vibrational signals acquired by inertial measurement units (IMUs) at six chest-lead positions from 17 subjects. Based on this dataset, we also provide a benchmark. Experimental results demonstrate the feasibility of reconstructing electrical cardiac signals at variable locations from vibrational signals using a lightweight 364 K-parameter U-Net. Furthermore, we observe a hallucination phenomenon in the model, where ECG waveforms are generated in regions where no corresponding electrical activity is present. We analyze the causes of this phenomenon and propose potential directions for mitigation. This study demonstrates the feasibility of mobile-device-friendly ECG monitoring through chest-lead ECG prediction from low-cost vibrational signals acquired using IMU sensors. It expands the application of cardiac vibrational signals and provides new insights into the spatial relationship between cardiac electrical and mechanical activities with spatial location variation.

摘要（中文）: 十二导联心电图（ECG）对心血管疾病诊断至关重要，但其在日常生活中的长期采集受到复杂且昂贵硬件的限制。 近期研究探索了从低成本心脏振动信号（如心冲击图（SCG））重建ECG，然而，由于缺乏数据集，现有方法仅限于肢体导联，而临床诊断需要包括胸前导联的多导联ECG。 在这项工作中，我们提出了Vib2ECG，这是首个配对的多通道心电机械信号数据集，它包含来自17名受试者的完整十二导联ECG和由惯性测量单元（IMU）在六个胸前导联位置采集的振动信号。 基于此数据集，我们也提供了一个基准。 实验结果表明，使用一个轻量级的364K参数U-Net，从不同位置的振动信号重建电心脏信号是可行的。 此外，我们观察到模型中存在一种幻觉现象，即在不存在相应电活动的位置生成ECG波形。 我们分析了这种现象的原因，并提出了潜在的缓解方向。 这项研究证明了通过从使用IMU传感器采集的低成本振动信号预测胸前导联ECG，实现对移动设备友好的ECG监测的可行性。 它扩展了心脏振动信号的应用，并为心脏电活动和机械活动之间的空间关系随位置变化提供了新的见解。

TLDR: The paper introduces Vib2ECG, a paired SCG-ECG dataset for reconstructing ECG from vibrational signals obtained from wearable IMUs, presenting a benchmark and demonstrating the feasibility of chest-lead ECG reconstruction with a U-Net model.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了Vib2ECG，一个配对的SCG-ECG数据集，用于从可穿戴IMU获取的振动信号重建ECG，提出了一个基准，并展示了使用U-Net模型重建胸导联ECG的可行性。

Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)

Authors: Guorui Lu, Xiaohui Cai, Todor Stefanov, Qinyu Chen
